title: Perf baseline suite: hyperfine + criterion for strict/hardened critical paths
description:
Extreme optimization mapping.

Deliverables:
- Define canonical benchmark commands for allocator, string hot paths, and membrane decision path.
- Capture p50/p95/p99 baseline snapshots per mode.

Acceptance:
- Baselines regenerate deterministically in CI-capable environment.
- Regressions are detectable with clear thresholds.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.
notes: 2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): perf_baseline_test (8 pass) + gate script. Enforced suites covered, regression thresholds match budget policy, regeneration procedure complete.
close_reason: Perf baseline suite operational. 8 harness tests pass. check_perf_baseline.sh validates baseline coverage, benchmark suites, percentile targets, and regression thresholds.

Generational arena + quarantine allocator: UAF detection with P=1, per-allocation generation counter, freed blocks held in quarantine, 16-byte SipHash fingerprint + 8-byte canary per allocation. Size classes + thread cache. Covers malloc/calloc/realloc/free family and all allocation-related symbols.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: Starting execution on bd-2x5 based on bv robot triage (top actionable P0). Immediate objective: unblock allocator E2E closure by resolving the global LD_PRELOAD startup/interposition hang noted in bd-2x5.5. I will drive a reproduce -> isolate -> patch -> verify loop with deterministic logs and explicit strict+hardened evidence.

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text: Execution update (2026-02-14): I completed a deep reproduce/isolate pass on the LD_PRELOAD allocator E2E timeout path with multiple strict/hardened trace sets. Current state: /bin/true now exits 0 in strict+hardened, allocator fixtures run and print PASS text in some runs, but processes still time out (exit 124) due recursive runtime-kernel/panic-hook interactions during interposed libc paths (observed around puts/strlen/write/posix_memalign and runtime_math observe/lock paths). I implemented guarded mitigations in runtime_policy + allocator/string guards and added runtime_policy unit tests; blocker remains unresolved for bd-2x5.5 and I attached next-step boundary in bd-2x5.5 comment.

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text: Latest isolate update (2026-02-14): allocator fixture logic now executes to PASS output under strict+hardened, but process-termination path remains stuck (timeout 124) with repeated runtime-kernel panic fallback messages. This narrows the epic blocker to shutdown/stdio interposition recursion rather than core allocation correctness; bd-2x5.5 remains blocked pending termination-path fail-closed fix.

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text:
Closure evidence refresh for bd-2x5 (2026-02-19): prior LD_PRELOAD termination blocker from 2026-02-14 no longer reproduces on current head.

Validation commands and outcomes:

1) Allocator E2E fixture parity vs host (strict + hardened)
- Command: `TIMEOUT_SECONDS=20 rch exec -- bash scripts/check_allocator_e2e.sh`
- Result: PASS
  - `[PASS] fixture_malloc host=0 strict=0 hardened=0 strict_match=True hardened_match=True`
  - `[PASS] fixture_malloc_stress host=0 strict=0 hardened=0 strict_match=True hardened_match=True`
- Artifacts:
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/report.json`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/host/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/strict/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc/hardened/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc_stress/host/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc_stress/strict/stdout.txt`
  - `target/allocator_e2e/20260219T192625Z/fixture_malloc_stress/hardened/stdout.txt`

2) Allocator core unit-test slice (arena/quarantine/cache invariants)
- Command: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core malloc::allocator -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`
- Result: PASS
  - 23 allocator tests passed, 0 failed
  - Includes lifecycle/invariant tests and realloc/calloc/thread-cache scenarios.

This satisfies the epicŌĆÖs outstanding closure blocker and provides deterministic strict+hardened evidence artifacts.

title: Allocator: Generational arena with quarantine zones
description: Implement generational arena: per-allocation generation counter, freed blocks held in quarantine before reclamation, quarantine sizing policy. UAF detection with P=1 guarantee. Must integrate with TSM fingerprint and canary stages.
notes: 2026-02-13 AzurePeak execution summary: Verified generational arena + quarantine implementation in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/arena.rs and strengthened coverage with two tests: (1) free_promotes_slot_to_quarantined_with_new_generation, (2) quarantine_drain_evicts_oldest_when_entry_count_exceeded. Validation command: cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane arena:: -- --nocapture (10 passed). Additional attempted command cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane ptr_validator::tests:: -- --nocapture currently fails on unrelated pre-existing compile issue in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs (unstable thread_id_value feature).
close_reason: Implemented and verified generational arena+quarantine behavior; added generation/state-transition and entry-count drain tests in arena.rs; targeted arena test suite passes.

title: Allocator: Size classes + thread-local cache
description: Implement size-class segregation and thread-local allocation cache for hot-path performance. Covers malloc/calloc/realloc/free/memalign/posix_memalign/aligned_alloc. Must maintain generation counters and quarantine invariants through all paths.
notes: 2026-02-13 AzurePeak execution summary: validated size-class + thread-cache allocator path in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/malloc/{size_class.rs,thread_cache.rs,allocator.rs}; confirmed overflow-to-central-bin coverage test (test_thread_cache_overflow_spills_to_central_bin_and_reuses) is present and passing in allocator tests. Validation commands: (1) cargo test -p frankenlibc-core malloc::allocator::tests:: -- --nocapture (19 passed), (2) cargo test -p frankenlibc-core malloc:: -- --nocapture (47 passed).
close_reason: Size-class and thread-cache behavior validated with passing allocator+malloc test suites; central-bin spill/reuse coverage confirmed.

title: Allocator: SipHash fingerprint + canary per allocation
description: 16-byte SipHash fingerprint in allocation header + 8-byte trailing canary. Used by TSM pipeline for corruption detection. P(undetected corruption) <= 2^-64. Must not increase hot-path allocation latency beyond budget.
notes: 2026-02-13 AzurePeak execution summary: validated fingerprint+canary pipeline in membrane. Confirmed 16-byte AllocationFingerprint header and 8-byte canary behavior via focused tests. Validation commands: (1) cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane fingerprint::tests:: -- --nocapture (6 passed), (2) cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane canary_corruption_detected -- --nocapture (2 passed: arena + ptr_validator canary corruption paths).
close_reason: Fingerprint header + canary behavior validated with focused membrane tests (roundtrip/verify/corruption paths).

title: Allocator: Unit tests - generation counters, quarantine, corruption detection
description: Unit tests: generation counter increment/wrap, quarantine fill/drain/sizing, SipHash fingerprint verification, canary integrity, double-free detection, UAF detection (P=1 must be demonstrated), foreign pointer rejection. Include proptest for randomized alloc/free sequences.
notes: 2026-02-13 AzurePeak execution summary: allocator/membrane unit-test closure validated across required invariants. Commands + outcomes: (1) cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane ptr_validator::tests:: -- --nocapture (10 passed: UAF/double-free/foreign-pointer/canary paths), (2) cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane arena::tests:: -- --nocapture (10 passed: generation/quarantine drain/corruption detection), (3) cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane fingerprint::tests:: -- --nocapture (6 passed: fingerprint/canary integrity). Complementary deterministic allocation-trace invariant tests were already validated in frankenlibc-core malloc suites during bd-2x5.2 closure.
close_reason: Required allocator/membrane unit-test coverage validated (generation/quarantine/fingerprint/canary/UAF/double-free/foreign-pointer paths all passing).

title: Allocator: E2E tests - concurrent workload, fragmentation, conformance vs glibc
description: E2E tests: concurrent alloc/free from N threads, fragmentation measurement over long runs, conformance differential against glibc (same allocation patterns must succeed/fail identically). Benchmark: allocation throughput vs jemalloc/mimalloc baselines. Must verify no UAF escapes under concurrent stress.
notes: Implemented allocator E2E differential scaffold and stress fixture: added tests/integration/fixture_malloc_stress.c, scripts/check_allocator_e2e.sh, and registered fixture in tests/conformance/c_fixture_spec.json (totals now 10 fixtures / 41 tests). Validation evidence: 1) TIMEOUT_SECONDS=25 scripts/check_allocator_e2e.sh -> host passes but strict/hardened timeout (exit 124) for fixture_malloc and fixture_malloc_stress; report at target/allocator_e2e/20260213T202935Z/report.json. 2) Minimal repro shows global interposition hang: timeout 5 env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict LD_PRELOAD=/data/projects/frankenlibc/target/allocator_e2e_cargo/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so /bin/true -> 124 (same for hardened). 3) strace repro captured repeated sched_yield loop before SIGTERM timeout (/tmp/frankenlibc_preload_true.strace). Bead is blocked pending resolution of global LD_PRELOAD startup/interpose regression in current tree.
close_reason: Allocator e2e strict+hardened now passes including stress fixture; verification and reporting complete.

If the assigned owner (AzurePeak) has additional repro data/artifacts, please add paths here and I will fold them into the fix+verification runbook.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text: Clarification: repro target is timeout 5 with FRANKENLIBC_MODE strict plus LD_PRELOAD libfrankenlibc_abi.so running /bin/true, which currently exits 124 and shows sched_yield looping in prior trace. I will publish before/after command logs and artifact paths once patched.

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text: Blocker detail (2026-02-14): allocator E2E still fails with strict/hardened exit=124 even after startup-mode and runtime-policy recursion mitigations. Repro: TIMEOUT_SECONDS=30 scripts/check_allocator_e2e.sh. Latest run_dir: target/allocator_e2e/20260214T052002Z. Signals: fixture stdout can reach PASS text, but process fails to terminate; stderr shows repeated 'frankenlibc: runtime kernel panic (fallback)' lines in strict+hardened. Focus area narrowed to interposed stdio/string/write termination path plus runtime_math panic/lock recursion under preload. Suggested next bead slice: isolate panic origin in runtime_math observe/decide for Stdio/termination paths, then add deterministic fail-closed gate that preserves process exit semantics.

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text: Follow-up isolate (2026-02-14): latest run still fails (run_dir target/allocator_e2e/20260214T052002Z). New signal: fixture_malloc strict/hardened stdout now reaches 'fixture_malloc: PASS (6 tests)' before timeout, but exit_code remains 124; stderr emits repeated 'frankenlibc: runtime kernel panic (fallback)'. Trace tail now points at termination/stdio path (puts/strlen/write/posix_memalign) rather than initial calloc startup. Remaining blocker appears to be panic-loop behavior after workload success, not allocator functional mismatch.

comments.author: PinkCat
comments.text: Progress update (2026-02-14): fixed teardown panic-loop regression for fixture_malloc by hardening TLS access fallbacks and panic-hook behavior. Key changes: runtime_policy panic hook now uses raw syscall write + bounded emission; runtime_policy allocator/string/pthread reentry guards now use try_with fallbacks; errno/TLS cache paths now avoid post-destruction TLS panics. Validation: fixture_malloc strict/hardened now exit 0 with no panic stderr; allocator_e2e run_dir=target/allocator_e2e/20260214T181738Z shows fixture_malloc PASS for host/strict/hardened. Remaining blocker: fixture_malloc_stress still times out (strict=124, hardened=124, host=0) with empty stderr, indicating separate threading-path stall. Next slice should isolate stress hang in pthread lifecycle/runtime-policy threading path.

comments.author: PinkCat

comments.text:

title: bd-2ry subtask: Clean-room setjmp/sigsetjmp semantics contract + scope ledger
description:
Background:
- setjmp/longjmp semantics are high-risk because they interact with control transfer, signal masks, and stack invariants.

Goal:
- Produce clean-room phase-scoped spec for setjmp/sigsetjmp/longjmp/siglongjmp behavior and constraints.

Deliverables:
1) Supported ABI semantics matrix (what is fully supported now vs deferred).
2) Signal-mask interaction contract.
3) Explicit non-goals and safety constraints for phase-1.
4) Support matrix wording and user-visible caveats.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Spec is implementation-ready and unambiguous.
- Deferred semantics are explicit and test-gated.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for spec table/parsing helpers.
- Structured logs for spec contract checks and matrix generation.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes: Implemented clean-room setjmp/sigsetjmp semantics contract + scope ledger with deterministic gate coverage. Added artifact tests/conformance/setjmp_semantics_contract.v1.json (phase-scoped ABI semantics matrix, signal-mask pairing rules, explicit phase-1 non-goals/safety constraints, support-matrix caveats). Added gate script scripts/check_setjmp_semantics_contract.sh that validates artifact/schema alignment against support_matrix.json, tests/conformance/stub_census.json, tests/conformance/stub_regression_waiver_policy.v1.json, and tests/conformance/fixtures/setjmp_ops.json; emits structured logs and report at target/conformance/setjmp_semantics_contract.{report.json,log.jsonl} plus tests/cve_arena/results/bd-2xp3/{trace.jsonl,artifact_index.json}. Added harness parser/validator + unit tests in crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/setjmp_contract.rs and integration gate test in crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/setjmp_semantics_contract_test.rs. Validation commands: scripts/check_setjmp_semantics_contract.sh; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/frankenlibc-target-rusticcoast cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness setjmp_contract -- --nocapture; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/frankenlibc-target-rusticcoast cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_semantics_contract_test -- --nocapture.
close_reason: Delivered setjmp/sigsetjmp phase-scoped semantics contract + deterministic gate/tests/log artifacts.

title: RaptorQ Runtime: Corruption/loss simulation tests (bit flips, drops)
description:
Add tests that simulate symbol loss/corruption and validate recovery behavior.

Tests:
- Drop up to R symbols -> decode succeeds.
- Drop beyond R -> decode fails with explainable reason.
- Bit flips -> checksum catches; decode either repairs or fails deterministically.

Perf:
- Ensure tests run in harness, not on strict hot path.
notes:
Implemented deterministic loss/corruption simulation tests in `glibc-rs-harness`.

Changes:
- Decoder policy hardened: `evidence_decode::decode_epoch` now ignores records that fail `validate_basic`, fail payload hash, or have epoch-param mismatch (seed/k/r). Chain-hash mismatches are still tolerated (gap detection).
- Added unit tests:
  - loss within redundancy: drop systematic records with loss <= R and assert full recovery
  - loss beyond redundancy: drop all repairs + one systematic and assert missing remains
  - bit flip: corrupt one systematic payload, assert payload hash mismatch is counted and decode recovers by ignoring corrupt record

All `glibc-rs-harness` tests pass; workspace quality gates (fmt/check/clippy/test --all-targets) were green as of this update.
close_reason: Added harness loss/corruption simulation tests + decoder skips payload-hash-invalid records

title: Membrane: TLS cache invalidation on free (prevent CachedValid UAF)
description:
Problem: ptr_validator TLS cache can return CachedValid after free because entries are never invalidated across threads, breaking UAF detection guarantees.

Acceptance:
- Add regression test: validate() -> CachedValid, free(), validate() must NOT return CachedValid and must deny read/write.
- Implement deterministic invalidation mechanism that works cross-thread (epoch/stamp) without allocations.
- Keep strict fast-path overhead minimal (single atomic load/compare per TLS lookup).
notes: Fixed TLS cache stale-hit-after-free bug by adding a global invalidation epoch stamp. Cache entries are tagged with epoch at insert and only hit when epochs match; AllocationArena::free bumps epoch for known pointers. Added regression test tls_cache_does_not_allow_uaf_after_free in ptr_validator.rs. Ran: rustfmt on touched files; cargo test -p glibc-rs-membrane.
close_reason: Added epoch-stamped TLS cache entries and bump-on-free invalidation to prevent stale CachedValid hits after free; added regression test; cargo test -p glibc-rs-membrane passes.

title: Drift: Fusion SIGNALS consistency (compile-time assertion + test)
description:
Prevent silent misalignment between fusion SIGNALS constant and the severity vector built in observe_validation_result().

Acceptance criteria:
- Compile-time assertion that array length equals SIGNALS.
- Unit test that adding a cached state requires incrementing SIGNALS.

Why:
- If lengths diverge, fusion weights become meaningless.

comments.author: DustyPuma
comments.text: Fusion SIGNALS consistency implemented. Changes: (1) Made fusion::SIGNALS pub const. (2) Replaced hardcoded 46 with fusion::SIGNALS in severity vector. (3) Introduced BASE_SEVERITY_LEN=25 and META_SEVERITY_LEN=21 named constants. (4) Added compile-time const assertion: fusion::SIGNALS == BASE_SEVERITY_LEN + META_SEVERITY_LEN. (5) Updated base_severity array to use BASE_SEVERITY_LEN. (6) Updated existing test needle to match new literals. (7) Added 2 new tests: fusion_signals_matches_severity_vector_assignments (counts severity[N] assignments, verifies == META_SEVERITY_LEN) and fusion_signals_const_assertion_exists (verifies const assertion is present). Now any change to SIGNALS, BASE_SEVERITY_LEN, META_SEVERITY_LEN, or the actual severity assignments that breaks consistency will fail at compile time (const assertion) or test time (assignment count).

title: bd-z84 subtask: Clean-room mutex semantics contract (NORMAL/ERRORCHECK/RECURSIVE + errno matrix)
description:
Background:
- pthread mutex behavior is a correctness and performance cornerstone; the current work needs explicit semantics to avoid hidden glibc coupling or undefined edge behavior.

Goal:
- Produce a clean-room mutex semantics contract for NORMAL, ERRORCHECK, and RECURSIVE mutexes (phase-scoped) mapped to strict/hardened behavior.

Deliverables:
1) State transition table for lock/trylock/unlock/destroy and owner tracking.
2) Attribute handling matrix and explicitly deferred features (robust, PI, process-shared if deferred).
3) Error mapping contract (EINVAL/EBUSY/EPERM/EDEADLK/etc.) per state.
4) Contention and fairness notes for futex path.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Contract is sufficient to implement mutex core without consulting legacy code.
- All unsupported semantics are explicit and reflected in support matrix policy.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for transition table and errno mapping helpers.
- Structured logs for validation runs including trace_id, operation, old_state, new_state, errno, timing.
notes:
Closed with clean-room mutex semantics contract + validation artifacts.

Delivered:
1) State transition table + owner relation model:
- Implemented contract enums and transition law in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mutex.rs:
  - MutexContractState, MutexContractOp, MutexContractOutcome
  - mutex_contract_transition(kind, state, op) for NORMAL/ERRORCHECK/RECURSIVE

2) Attribute handling matrix + deferred features:
- Added MutexAttributeContract, mutex_attr_is_supported, mutex_attr_support_errno
- Explicitly deferred with deterministic EINVAL: process-shared, robust, PI, PP

3) Error mapping contract:
- Deterministic mappings for EINVAL/EBUSY/EPERM/EDEADLK encoded and tested
- Added missing core errno constant: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/errno/mod.rs (EDEADLK = 35)

4) Contention/fairness notes:
- Added futex_contention_fairness_note() contract note
- Added human-readable contract doc: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mutex_contract.md

Verification + structured logs:
- Unit tests in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mutex.rs cover transition/errno matrix behaviors.
- Added deterministic integration log/report emitter:
  - crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/pthread_mutex_contract_matrix_test.rs
  - JSONL artifact: target/conformance/pthread_mutex_contract_matrix.log.jsonl
  - Report: target/conformance/pthread_mutex_contract_matrix.report.json
  - log fields include trace_id, mode, operation, old_state, new_state, errno, timing_ns

Commands run:
- cargo fmt --all
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core pthread::mutex -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core --test pthread_mutex_contract_matrix_test -- --nocapture

Support-matrix policy alignment:
- pthread_mutex_* entries already updated in support_matrix.json to explicit implemented semantics with unsupported-layout handling.

title: TSM: Unit tests - validation pipeline stages
description:
Unit tests for each TSM validation pipeline stage in isolation: null-check correctness, TLS cache hit/miss/eviction, bloom filter false-positive rate bounds, arena lookup accuracy, fingerprint verification, canary integrity, bounds enforcement. Must include property-based tests (proptest) for pipeline composition. Must verify early-exit behavior and structured trace emission.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

title: Evidence/logging unification v2: schema, joins, and artifact index integrity
description:
Background:
- Existing logging contracts are solid, but closure quality depends on consistent evidence across unit/e2e/perf/release workflows.

Goal:
- Ensure one canonical structured evidence schema is emitted by all verification surfaces and can be machine-joined across artifacts.

Deliverables:
1) Unified schema profile for unit/e2e/perf/release events.
2) Cross-artifact correlation keys (trace_id, bead_id/work-item, mode, api_family/symbol).
3) Artifact index verifier ensuring every failed test points to concrete diagnostics.
4) CI gate that validates schema conformance and required field completeness.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every failing path in CI emits parseable evidence with linkable artifacts.
- Schema validator catches missing/invalid fields deterministically.
- Downstream reports can aggregate by trace_id without lossy joins.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for schema serialization/validation and index generation.
- E2E tests that intentionally trigger representative failures and verify evidence completeness.
- Mandatory detailed logs for both success and failure paths.

Alien-artifact Explainability Requirements:
- Evidence records must support decision explainability (which controller/gate/action and why), not just pass/fail telemetry.
close_reason: All child deliverables validated; structured-log fixture/gate regression fixed and evidence compliance/tests passing.

comments.author: AmberStone
comments.text:
Closure audit (AmberStone, 2026-02-13): parent `bd-33p` revalidation uncovered and resolved a real schema-gate regression.

Issue found:
- `bash scripts/check_structured_logs.sh` failed because it validated intentionally-negative parser fixtures (`tests/gentoo/fixtures/sample_logs/invalid_*.jsonl`) as if they were required-valid logs.
- Also, the "valid" sample fixtures were missing schema-required fields (`trace_id`, `level`, and in runtime fixture explicit `timestamp` field where only legacy `ts` was present).

Fix applied:
1. Updated `scripts/check_structured_logs.sh` to skip only negative fixture files under `tests/gentoo/fixtures/sample_logs/invalid_*.jsonl`.
2. Updated valid fixtures to satisfy structured-log required fields while preserving parser semantics:
   - `tests/gentoo/fixtures/sample_logs/valid_runtime.jsonl`
   - `tests/gentoo/fixtures/sample_logs/valid_hook.jsonl`

Verification (all PASS):
1. `bash scripts/check_structured_logs.sh`
2. `bash scripts/check_evidence_compliance.sh`
3. `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test structured_log_test --test evidence_compliance_test`
4. `python3 -m unittest tests/gentoo/test_log_parser.py -q`

Result: schema/join/compliance gates are green with deterministic handling of negative fixtures and updated valid sample logs.

title: Integration: real-world application testing
description:
Final integration testing with real-world applications.

Goal: Validate FrankenLibC works correctly with production software.

Test Applications (Tier 1 - Must Work):
1. bash - Interactive shell, job control
2. coreutils - ls, cat, cp, mv, grep, etc.
3. Python 3 - Interpreter, pip, basic scripts
4. curl - HTTP/HTTPS requests
5. git - Version control operations

Test Applications (Tier 2 - Should Work):
1. nginx - HTTP server, static files
2. PostgreSQL - Database server
3. Redis - Key-value store
4. Node.js - JavaScript runtime
5. Go programs - Static binaries

Test Scenarios:
1. Basic functionality
2. Long-running operation
3. High-load stress test
4. Error condition handling
5. Graceful shutdown

Validation:
- Functional correctness (same output as glibc)
- Performance (within acceptable overhead)
- Stability (no crashes over extended run)
- Memory (no leaks, bounded overhead)

Infrastructure:
- Docker containers for each application
- Automated test scripts
- Output comparison framework
- Performance monitoring

Success Criteria:
- All Tier 1 applications work correctly
- Tier 2 applications work for basic operations
- No crashes or hangs
- Performance within budgets

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.

- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
## Integration Test Results ŌĆö RusticHorse (2026-03-22)

### 45+ programs work under LD_PRELOAD

Working: true, echo, cat, ls, pwd, date, uname, id, whoami, wc, head, tail, 
stat, env, basename, dirname, expr, seq, md5sum, sha256sum, find, tty, 
realpath, readlink, touch, rm, mkdir, rmdir, cp, mv, chmod, free, yes, nl,

title: Epic: Formal Proof Obligations (22 Theorems)

22 formal proof obligations required before release: strict refinement, hardened safety, deterministic replay, CPOMDP safety feasibility, barrier invariance, HJI viability, sheaf global-consistency, SOS certificate soundness, Galois connection safety model (gamma(alpha(c))>=c), monotonic safety (lattice join idempotent), P(undetected corruption)<=2^-64, UAF detection P=1, buffer overflow P(miss)<=2^-64. title: scanf scan_char: unchecked pos+n add panics / undefined on huge width
description: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/scanf.rs:965 computes 'if pos + n > input.len()' where n is spec.width.unwrap_or(1). If the format specifies an enormous width (fuzzer crafted, or pathological user input), pos + n overflows usize. In debug mode this panics (fuzz_scanf surfaced it); in release mode it's UB ŌĆö could wrap around and skip the bounds check, reading past input. Fix: use pos.checked_add(n) and return None on overflow.
notes: Fixed in 6d29fb13: scan_char uses pos.checked_add(n) with early-return None on overflow. Protects against fuzzer/pathological spec.width values.

title: [MEDIUM] fuzz: stateful pidfd and timer differential harness
description:
[MEDIUM] fuzz: add stateful pidfd/timer differential harness

Add a stateful differential fuzz target in `crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/` for syscall wrappers whose behavior depends on handle lifecycle and sequencing.

Recommended syscall set:
- `pidfd_open` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:5055`)
- `pidfd_send_signal` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:5067`)
- `timer_settime` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:7569`)
- `timer_gettime` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:7592`)

Fuzz shape:
- generate short action sequences instead of single calls
- model create/use/close/delete/reuse transitions
- compare FrankenLibC vs host libc on errno, return values, and stale-handle behavior
- bias toward null pointers, deleted handles, invalid signal numbers, and mixed valid/invalid sequencing

Why now:
- recent backlog work added many focused parity tests for deleted handles and invalid inputs, but sequence-sensitive regressions will hide between those single-case pins.
close_reason: Verified fuzz_pidfd_timer stateful harness per spec: crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pidfd_timer.rs covers pidfd_open, pidfd_send_signal, timer_create, timer_settime, timer_gettime, timer_getoverrun, timer_delete with a handle table that tracks live vs stale ids and asserts rc matches the lifecycle bucket. Safety: pid restricted to 0 / self / nonexistent, signals restricted to {0, SIGWINCH, SIGURG} to avoid terminating the fuzz binary, handle table capped at 8 to prevent fd/timer exhaustion. Registered in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml; corpus dir crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/corpus/fuzz_pidfd_timer/ has 752 seeds. Build verified: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_fuzz cargo +nightly build --manifest-path crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_pidfd_timer -> Finished dev profile in 0.48s (clean). Work landed in commit 2548d741 (feat(fuzz): add fuzz_pidfd_timer stateful handle-lifecycle harness).

title: Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build
description:
`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. However, `scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh` only scans `*_abi.rs` files, missing `host_resolve.rs` entirely. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.

To fix:
1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).
2. Update `build.rs` to respect `source_scan_exceptions` so `host_resolve::dlvsym` (and any future exceptions) can be whitelisted.
close_reason: Current tree already aligned: standalone build and replacement-guard parity test both pass

title: RaptorQ Runtime: Architecture decision (where codec lives; feature gates; strict/hardened behavior)
description:
Decide how RaptorQ functionality can exist in libc runtime without violating constraints.

Decisions required:
- Do we implement a minimal encoder in glibc-rs-membrane (no asupersync dep) and decode offline in harness?
- Or do we fully feature-gate a codec and keep it out of default builds?
- What is the object model? (Evidence object per epoch; metadata capsule objects)
- What symbol size T and max K do we allow?

Acceptance criteria:

- Clear rule: strict mode must not pay codec costs on hot path.

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text:
## Architecture Decision Checklist (RaptorQ In Runtime)

This bead exists to force explicit decisions before we write code that is hard to unwind.

### Decision 1: Codec Choice (Runtime vs Tooling)
We must pick one of these *and document why*:
1. **Runtime implements a tiny XOR-only fountain encoder** (LT-like).
   - Pros: simplest, fastest, no GF(256)
   - Cons: weaker failure curves than full RaptorQ; tooling must match encoder exactly
2. **Runtime implements RaptorQ-compatible schedule (encoding only)**.
   - Pros: tooling can reuse asupersyncŌĆÖs RFC6330 implementation for decode/proofs
   - Cons: still some complexity (tuple generator, partitioning); more code in libc
3. **Runtime implements only systematic, no repair**, and tooling accepts loss.
   - Pros: minimal overhead
   - Cons: defeats the point; likely insufficient for ŌĆ£alien artifactŌĆØ evidence

Constraint reminder: libc runtime must not depend on `/dp/asupersync`.

### Decision 2: Strict vs Hardened Behavior
- Strict mode:
  - must not do repair-symbol generation
  - may record only minimal systematic records (or even only counters)
- Hardened mode:
  - may generate repair symbols on cadence
  - may enable evidence export hooks

We must state whether strict records are emitted at all, and if so: size, rate, and where stored.

### Decision 3: Object Model / Epoching
Define:
- What is an ŌĆ£epochŌĆØ (by time, by count, by phase boundary, by thread)?
- What is `K_source` max per epoch?
- What is symbol size `T` (fixed-size evidence records vs varlen + padding)?

Hard requirement for determinism:
- epoch_id construction and seed derivation are stable and versioned.

### Decision 4: Storage / Export Surface
Choose storage strategy:
- purely in-memory ring buffer (default)
- optional `memfd`/mmap-backed buffer for post-mortem capture
- opt-in file emission (debug/harness only)

We must define rotation policy and failure behavior when buffers fill.

### Decision 5: Integrity and Tamper Evidence
Minimum:
- payload hash per record (xxh3_128 or blake3)
- hash-chain across records inside an epoch

Optional:
- keyed MAC (tooling-controlled) if we need adversarial tamper resistance

### Decision 6: Proof Story
- What constitutes a ŌĆ£DecodeProofŌĆØ for libc evidence?
  - ESI set used, reconstructed ranges, integrity checks, chain verification
- Which events must *always* produce proofs when repaired?

### Acceptance Criteria
- Written ADR-style decision (in bead comment or linked doc) that nails the above.
- Explicit perf budget statement for strict/hardened.
- Clear compatibility promise: schema versioning and forward/backward decode behavior.

title: EPIC: RCU for Thread-Local Hot Metadata [Score 8.0, section 14.8]
description:
Implement userspace Read-Copy-Update (RCU) using the QSBR (Quiescent State Based Reclamation) variant for lock-free reads of thread-local hot metadata. Motivation: The read-heavy metadata path is a persistent performance hotspot in profiling. Thread metadata (thread ID, stack bounds, TLS base, arena assignment, size-class cache) is read on every allocation and free but written rarely (only on thread creation/destruction or arena rebalancing). Current implementation uses mutex-based access which creates unnecessary contention on read-heavy paths. QSBR RCU provides: (1) Zero-overhead reads ŌĆö no atomic operations, no memory barriers on the read path. Readers simply access the current version of metadata directly. (2) Safe reclamation ŌĆö writers create a new version, publish it via atomic pointer swap, then wait for all readers to pass through a quiescent state before freeing the old version. (3) Uses: thread metadata structs, allocation size-class tables (read on every malloc to determine which size class), TLS cache entries. Implementation: QSBR is ideal because libc controls the quiescent state boundaries ŌĆö every syscall return and every allocation entry/exit is a natural quiescent point. Target: reduce metadata read latency from ~15ns (mutex acquire/release) to <2ns (direct pointer read).

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.8 of graveyard (Score 8.0). QSBR RCU from Desnoyers et al. (2012). McKenney (2004) RCU survey. Userspace RCU (liburcu) as reference.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Core RcuDomain<T> type in src/concurrency/rcu.rs.
- QSBR integration points at malloc/free entry/exit and syscall boundaries.
- Migration from mutex to RCU via shadow-mode validation.
- Benchmark infrastructure comparing RCU vs mutex vs other primitives.
acceptance_criteria:
## Success Criteria
1. QSBR RCU primitive implemented and passing correctness tests.
2. Metadata read latency reduced from ~15ns to <2ns.
3. Migration completed for ThreadInfo, SizeClassTable, and TlsCache.
4. Benchmark demonstrates near-linear read scaling vs mutex contention collapse.
5. TSan clean across all migrated paths.
6. Grace period overhead documented and acceptable (<10us at p99).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- RCU lifecycle events at debug level. Grace period timing at debug level.
- Benchmark results as structured JSON artifacts.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

title: Implement QSBR-based RCU for thread metadata
description: Implement the core QSBR (Quiescent State Based Reclamation) RCU primitive for FrankenLibC. Components: (1) rcu_read_lock() / rcu_read_unlock() ŌĆö no-ops in QSBR (readers are implicitly in a read-side critical section between quiescent states). (2) rcu_quiescent_state() ŌĆö called at natural quiescent points (syscall boundaries, allocation entry/exit). Updates the per-thread epoch counter. (3) synchronize_rcu() ŌĆö blocks until all threads have passed through at least one quiescent state since the call began. Implementation: per-thread epoch counter (cache-line aligned to avoid false sharing), global epoch counter. Writer increments global epoch, then spins until all registered thread epochs >= global epoch. (4) call_rcu(callback) ŌĆö deferred version: enqueues callback to be invoked after grace period. (5) rcu_register_thread() / rcu_unregister_thread() ŌĆö track which threads are participating in RCU. Must handle thread death gracefully (dead threads are implicitly quiescent). Data structures: RcuDomain<T> wrapping an AtomicPtr<T> for the published data, with read() returning &T (lifetime tied to read-side critical section). All unsafe code must be documented with safety invariants.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.8 of graveyard (Score 8.0). QSBR RCU from Desnoyers et al. (2012). McKenney (2004) RCU survey. Userspace RCU (liburcu) as reference implementation.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- RcuDomain<T>: wraps AtomicPtr<T> with read()/update()/synchronize() interface.
- Per-thread epoch counter: #[repr(align(64))] EpochCounter(AtomicU64) to avoid false sharing.
- Global epoch: AtomicU64 with Relaxed load on read path, Release store on write path.
- rcu_quiescent_state(): called at malloc/free entry/exit, syscall return. Single Relaxed store.
- synchronize_rcu(): increment global epoch, spin until all thread epochs >= global. Yield between scans.
- Thread registration: ThreadLocal<EpochCounter> with Drop impl for automatic unregistration.
- All unsafe code blocks documented with SAFETY comments referencing QSBR invariants.
acceptance_criteria:
1. RcuDomain<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/update cycle.
2. Read path is zero-overhead: no atomic RMW, no memory barrier (verified by inspecting assembly with cargo asm).
3. synchronize_rcu() correctly waits for all readers (test: reader holds reference across grace period boundary).
4. Thread death handled correctly: dead threads do not block synchronize_rcu() indefinitely.
5. Concurrent stress test: 16 readers + 1 writer, 1M operations, no data races (TSan clean).
6. Read latency <2ns at p99 (vs ~15ns for mutex baseline).
7. Grace period latency <10us at p99 under 16-thread workload.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: rcu, operation=read, domain, epoch) on read (disabled in release builds).
- tracing::debug!(target: rcu, operation=synchronize, domain, global_epoch, grace_period_us) on synchronize.
- tracing::info!(target: rcu, thread_registered/thread_unregistered, thread_id) on registration events.

comments.author: PurpleHawk
comments.text:
DONE: QSBR-based RCU implementation complete. Module: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/rcu/mod.rs (527 lines + tests).

Components:
- ReaderSlot (cache-line aligned per-thread state)
- rcu_register_thread / rcu_unregister_thread (allocation-free open-addressed table, MAX_RCU_THREADS=256)
- rcu_quiescent_state (single atomic store at quiescent points)
- rcu_read_lock / rcu_read_unlock (no-ops in QSBR)
- synchronize_rcu (increment global epoch, spin until all readers catch up)
- call_rcu (deferred callback queue, CALLBACK_QUEUE_CAP=256)
- process_rcu_callbacks (invokes callbacks whose grace period elapsed)
- RcuDomain<T> (type-safe RCU-protected pointer with read/update)

Design choices:
- Allocation-free: static arrays following TLS table pattern
- Cache-line padded reader slots to avoid false sharing
- EPOCH_OFFLINE sentinel for unregistered/offline threads
- Writers serialize externally (no internal mutex)

Tests: 23 tests covering registration, quiescent states, synchronize_rcu (single/multi-reader), RcuDomain lifecycle, call_rcu deferred callbacks, min_reader_epoch tracking, and end-to-end grace period verification.

All tests pass (both single-threaded and parallel). cargo check --all-targets clean. Closing.

title: Replace mutex-based metadata reads with RCU
description: Migrate all thread-local hot metadata reads from mutex-based access to the QSBR RCU primitive. Targets: (1) Thread metadata struct (ThreadInfo: tid, stack_base, stack_size, tls_base, arena_id) ŌĆö currently protected by a per-thread mutex, read on every TSM validation. Replace with RcuDomain<ThreadInfo>. Writers (thread creation/destruction) use synchronize_rcu() after publishing new metadata. (2) Size-class lookup table ŌĆö currently a global RwLock<SizeClassTable>. Replace with RcuDomain<SizeClassTable>. Table is updated only when the allocator reconfigures size classes (rare). (3) TLS cache entries ŌĆö per-thread allocation cache. Replace mutex guard with RCU-protected snapshot. Migration strategy: (a) Add RCU wrapper alongside existing mutex. (b) Run both paths in shadow mode, comparing results. (c) Remove mutex path once RCU path is validated. (d) Verify with TSan that no data races exist. Each migration point must have a before/after benchmark showing latency improvement.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.8. RCU migration patterns from McKenney (2013) Perfbook. Shadow-mode validation from Hosek & Cadar (2015).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Migration wrapper: RcuMigration<T> that runs both mutex and RCU paths in parallel during migration.
- Phase 1: Add RCU alongside mutex. Both paths execute; results compared (debug assert on mismatch).
- Phase 2: RCU primary, mutex disabled. TSan verification.
- Phase 3: Remove mutex path entirely.
- Migration targets: ThreadInfo (per-thread metadata), SizeClassTable (global), TlsCache (per-thread).
- Before/after benchmark captured per migration point as structured JSON artifact.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Shadow-mode comparison confirms zero mismatches across 1M operations per migration target.
2. TSan clean after mutex path removal (all 3 targets).
3. ThreadInfo read latency drops from ~15ns to <2ns (7x improvement).
4. SizeClassTable read latency drops from ~20ns to <3ns.
5. No regression in write-path latency (grace period overhead acceptable).
6. Migration rollback works: can re-enable mutex path via feature flag without code changes.
7. Before/after benchmark artifacts committed to benchmarks/rcu_migration/ directory.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::info!(target: rcu_migration, target, phase, mutex_result, rcu_result, match) during shadow mode.
- tracing::warn!(target: rcu_migration, target, mismatch, mutex_result, rcu_result) on shadow mismatch.
- Migration completion: tracing::info!(target: rcu_migration, target, phase=complete, latency_before_ns, latency_after_ns).
close_reason: Delivered: code + artifacts present.

title: Benchmark: RCU vs mutex for metadata read throughput
description:
Comprehensive benchmark comparing RCU-based metadata reads vs mutex-based reads across multiple contention scenarios. Test matrix: (1) Thread counts: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64 threads. (2) Read/write ratios: 100:0 (read-only), 99:1, 95:5, 90:10, 50:50. (3) Operations: thread metadata lookup, size-class table lookup, TLS cache read. (4) Metrics per scenario: throughput (ops/sec), p50/p95/p99 latency, CPU utilization, cache miss rate (via perf stat). Expected results: RCU should show near-linear read scaling (reads are wait-free) while mutex shows contention collapse beyond 8 threads. Write path may be slightly slower with RCU (must wait for grace period). Break-even point analysis: at what read/write ratio does RCU become faster than mutex? Generate: (a) Throughput vs thread count graphs. (b) Latency distribution histograms. (c) Before/after flamegraphs showing eliminated lock contention. (d) Structured JSON results for CI regression tracking.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.8. Benchmarking methodology from McKenney (2013). Read-scaling analysis from Desnoyers et al. (2012).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- BenchMatrix struct defining thread_counts, rw_ratios, operations per test configuration.
- Use criterion.rs groups with parameterized benchmarks: criterion::BenchmarkGroup::new(thread_count, rw_ratio).
- perf stat integration: cache miss rates via perf-event crate or subprocess perf stat -e L1-dcache-load-misses.
- Output: structured JSON + gnuplot-compatible .dat files for graphing.
- Pin threads to specific cores via sched_setaffinity for reproducibility.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Benchmark covers full matrix: 7 thread counts x 5 RW ratios x 3 operations = 105 configurations.
2. RCU shows near-linear read scaling (throughput increases proportionally with thread count for read-only).
3. Mutex shows contention collapse beyond 8 threads for read-heavy workloads.
4. Break-even analysis documented: RW ratio threshold where RCU becomes faster than mutex.
5. Throughput vs thread count graph generated as SVG.
6. Latency distribution histograms generated for p50/p95/p99.
7. Results reproducible: <10% variance between runs (coefficient of variation).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- Per configuration: JSON record with thread_count, rw_ratio, ops_per_sec, p50_ns, p95_ns, p99_ns, cache_miss_rate.
- Summary: rcu_vs_mutex_benchmark.json with all configurations and break-even analysis.
- Flamegraphs: rcu_contention.svg and mutex_contention.svg for visual comparison.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

title: LCRQ ring queues for IPC (section 14.6)
description:
Implement LCRQ (Linked Concurrent Ring Queue) for high-performance inter-process communication in FrankenLibC. LCRQ combines the cache-friendliness of ring buffers with the unbounded capacity of linked lists. Structure: a linked list of fixed-size ring segments. Each segment is a cache-line-aligned array with head/tail indices using FAA (Fetch-And-Add) for O(1) enqueue/dequeue. When a segment fills, a new one is linked. Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Evidence ledger IPC ŌĆö when the evidence ledger runs in a separate process (for crash isolation), LCRQ provides the high-throughput, low-latency communication channel. Target: >10M events/sec with <100ns per-event latency. (2) Deferred free IPC ŌĆö in hardened mode, freed blocks may be sent to a separate quarantine manager process. LCRQ handles the transfer without blocking the application thread. (3) Metric export ŌĆö real-time allocation statistics exported to monitoring via LCRQ. Implementation: (a) LcrqSegment<T, N> ŌĆö ring buffer of N slots. (b) Lcrq<T, N> ŌĆö linked list of segments with hazard-pointer-based reclamation for consumed segments. (c) Shared memory support ŌĆö LCRQ must work across processes via mmap'd shared memory (all pointers must be offset-based, not absolute). (d) Backpressure ŌĆö when the consumer falls behind, the producer sees a full queue and applies configurable backpressure (block, drop, or signal).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.6 of graveyard. LCRQ from Morrison & Afek (2013 PPoPP). Linked ring queue combining CRQ segments for unbounded MPMC queues. Hazard pointer reclamation from Michael (2004).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- LcrqSegment<T, const N: usize>: ring buffer of N slots, head/tail as AtomicU64 using fetch_add.
- Lcrq<T, const N: usize>: linked list of segments. New segment allocated when current fills.
- Slot: enum { Empty, Full(T), Invalid }. CAS-based enqueue/dequeue on individual slots.
- Shared memory: offset-based pointers (usize offsets from base) for cross-process use via mmap.
- Hazard pointer: reclaim consumed segments after grace period (integrate with RCU from bd-3aof if available).
- Backpressure: BackpressurePolicy enum { Block, Drop, Signal } configurable per queue instance.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. LCRQ passes MPMC correctness test: 16 producers + 16 consumers, 1M items, zero lost items.
2. Throughput >10M items/sec for 64-byte payloads on single-socket system.
3. Per-item latency <100ns at p99 under moderate load (50% utilization).
4. Shared memory mode: cross-process producer/consumer works correctly (test with fork()).
5. Backpressure: Block mode blocks producer when consumer falls behind, Drop mode drops with counter, Signal mode sends SIGUSR2.
6. Segment reclamation: no memory leak after 10M enqueue/dequeue cycles.
7. Miri clean for single-threaded correctness path.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: lcrq, operation=enqueue|dequeue, segment_id, slot_index, backpressure) per operation.
- tracing::debug!(target: lcrq, segment_allocated|segment_reclaimed, segment_id, total_segments) on segment lifecycle.
- Periodic: queue_depth, throughput_ops_per_sec, backpressure_events via tracing::info every 100K ops.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.
close_reason: Delivered: code + artifacts present.

title: CI: comprehensive pipeline integration
description:
Comprehensive CI pipeline for all quality gates.

Goal: Automated CI that validates all FrankenLibC quality gates.

CI Jobs:

1. Build Matrix
   - cargo build --release (all targets)
   - cargo build --release -p frankenlibc-abi
   - cargo build --features=standalone (L2+)

2. Quality Gates
   - cargo fmt --check
   - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
   - cargo test --all-targets

3. Conformance Suite
   - scripts/ci.sh (basic gate)
   - scripts/conformance_golden_gate.sh
   - scripts/snapshot_gate.sh

4. LD_PRELOAD Smoke
   - scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh (strict)
   - FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh

5. Performance Gates
   - cargo bench -p frankenlibc-bench
   - Strict <20ns validation
   - Hardened <200ns validation
   - No regressions vs baseline

6. CVE Arena
   - All CVE triggers pass in hardened mode
   - Metrics capture for evidence

7. Fuzzing (nightly)
   - 1M iterations per fuzz target
   - Crash reporting
   - Coverage report

8. Multi-arch (when available)
   - aarch64 cross-compile
   - QEMU test run

Artifacts:
- Build logs
- Test results (JUnit XML)
- Coverage reports
- Benchmark results
- Fuzzing coverage

Success Criteria:
- All gates pass on every PR
- Performance baselines maintained
- Coverage reports generated
- Nightly fuzzing runs

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.

title: EPIC: Extreme Optimization Loop (baselines + profiles + goldens)
description:
Enforce the extreme-software-optimization loop for allocator + membrane:
BASELINE -> PROFILE -> PROVE -> IMPLEMENT (one lever) -> VERIFY -> REPEAT.

Deliverables:
- Baseline suite (hyperfine + criterion).
- Flamegraph/profiler playbook.
- Golden outputs + isomorphism proof template wired into PR process.

Acceptance:
- We can prove behavior unchanged after optimizations via goldens/checksums.
- Perf regressions are detected quickly and reproducibly.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

title: Support matrix: automated maintenance
description:
Automated support matrix maintenance.

Goal: Keep support_matrix.json synchronized with code reality.

Automation Components:

1. Symbol Discovery
   - Parse exported symbols from libfrankenlibc_abi.so
   - Compare against support_matrix.json
   - Report new/removed symbols

2. Status Validation
   - For Implemented: verify no todo!/unimplemented!
   - For RawSyscall: verify syscall veneer usage
   - For GlibcCallThrough: verify glibc call
   - For Stub: verify deterministic fallback

3. Conformance Linkage
   - Each symbol links to fixture files
   - Each symbol links to test commands
   - Coverage report generation

4. Drift Detection
   - CI gate fails on drift
   - PR comments with drift summary
   - Automated fix suggestions

5. Report Generation
   - reality_report.v1.json from support_matrix
   - Feature parity reports
   - Coverage dashboards

CI Integration:
- Run on every PR
- Block merge on drift
- Generate coverage reports

Success Criteria:
- Zero manual maintenance of support_matrix
- Drift detected within one PR
- Reports always current

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.

title: EPIC: Methodology and Infrastructure
description:
Infrastructure and methodology beads that support the overall FrankenLibC development process. Covers: baseline performance capture for all 250 symbols, evidence ledger extensions for safety decisions, property-based testing framework, CI artifact gates, and decision contracts for strict/hardened mode. These are the foundational capabilities that make all other epics possible ŌĆö without baseline measurements, you cannot prove optimization. Without evidence ledger, you cannot audit safety decisions. Without property-based testing, you cannot find edge cases. Without CI gates, you cannot prevent regressions.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Methodology and infrastructure foundations from CMM/CMMI Level 3+ practices. Measurement-driven development from Basili & Weiss (1984) GQM paradigm.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Baseline infrastructure: criterion.rs benchmark suite in benches/.
- Evidence ledger: src/evidence/ledger.rs with append-only storage.
- Property-based testing: proptest strategies in tests/properties/.
- CI gates: .github/workflows/gates.yml orchestrating all quality checks.
- Decision contract: src/tsm/decision_contract.rs as const state machine.
acceptance_criteria:
## Success Criteria
1. Baseline capture operational for all 250 symbols.
2. Evidence ledger extended with galaxy-brain card format.
3. Property-based tests covering all 6 ABI families.
4. CI artifact gates enforced on all PRs.
5. Decision contract implemented and verified.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- Each subtask produces structured JSON artifacts for CI consumption and historical tracking.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

title: Capture baseline p50/p95/p99 for all 250 implemented symbols
description: Capture comprehensive baseline performance measurements for all 250 currently implemented symbols. For each symbol: (1) Design representative input vectors (small/medium/large inputs, aligned/unaligned for memory ops, short/long strings for string ops). (2) Measure with criterion.rs: p50, p95, p99 latency in nanoseconds. (3) Measure in 3 configurations: raw (no TSM), strict mode, hardened mode. (4) Record: CPU cycles (rdtsc), wall clock (criterion), instructions retired (perf stat), cache misses (perf stat). (5) Store as structured JSON artifacts with: symbol name, ABI family, input description, measurement timestamp, system info (CPU model, frequency, kernel version). (6) Generate summary report: per-family latency distribution, hotspot identification (symbols where strict overhead > 20ns or hardened overhead > 200ns), optimization opportunity ranking. This baseline is the foundation for all future optimization work ŌĆö you cannot improve what you do not measure. CI integration: baseline must be re-captured on each release candidate and compared against previous release.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Performance measurement methodology from Mytkowicz et al. (2009). Criterion.rs statistical framework. Benchmark design from Curtsinger & Berger (2013) Stabilizer.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- SymbolBenchSuite: macro that generates criterion benchmarks for all 250 symbols.
- Three configs: raw (no TSM), strict, hardened. Each measured independently.
- System info collection: /proc/cpuinfo parsing, uname -r, lscpu for L1/L2/L3 cache sizes.
- Output: symbol_baselines.json with per-symbol, per-config p50/p95/p99 in nanoseconds.
- CI: cargo bench --bench baseline_capture -- --output-format=json, archived as release artifact.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 250 symbols benchmarked in all 3 configurations (750 benchmark points).
2. Each benchmark statistically significant: coefficient of variation <5% across 3 runs.
3. Hotspot identification: symbols with strict_overhead > 20ns flagged in report.
4. Per-family summary: average overhead for string, stdlib, math, stdio, thread families.
5. Baseline stored as versioned JSON artifact with system fingerprint.
6. CI comparison: new baseline compared against previous, regressions >10% flagged.
7. Benchmark completes in <30 minutes on CI runner (parallelized across symbol families).
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- Per-symbol: JSON record with symbol, family, config, p50_ns, p95_ns, p99_ns, iterations, measurement_time_s.
- Summary: baseline_summary.json with family averages, hotspot list, system info.
- CI diff: baseline_comparison.json with symbol, previous, current, delta_pct, verdict.
close_reason: Delivered: code + artifacts present.

title: Extend EvidenceLedger for safety decisions (galaxy-brain cards)
description: Extend the EvidenceLedger system to record safety-critical decisions with full provenance, implementing 'galaxy-brain cards' ŌĆö structured decision records that capture the reasoning behind each safety-relevant choice. Each galaxy-brain card contains: (1) Decision ID ŌĆö unique, monotonically increasing. (2) Decision Type ŌĆö mode_transition, quarantine, repair, escalation, certificate_evaluation, policy_override. (3) Context ŌĆö the full state at decision time: thread ID, call stack, TSM state, active certificates, pending operations. (4) Reasoning ŌĆö the chain of evidence that led to the decision: which checks passed/failed, which certificates were evaluated, what the CPOMDP policy recommended. (5) Outcome ŌĆö what action was taken and its result. (6) Counterfactual ŌĆö what would have happened under the alternative decision (for post-hoc analysis). (7) Timestamp ŌĆö monotonic clock, not wall clock (deterministic). Storage: append-only, crash-safe (fsync after each batch). Query interface: filter by decision type, time range, thread, symbol. Export: structured JSON for CI analysis. The ledger enables: (a) post-incident forensics, (b) safety audit trails, (c) decision policy tuning based on historical outcomes.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Decision provenance from Pasquier et al. (2017). Audit trail design from Anderson (2008) Security Engineering. Galaxy-brain card format inspired by ADR (Architecture Decision Records) from Nygard (2011).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- EvidenceLedger struct: append-only Vec<DecisionCard> backed by memory-mapped file for crash safety.
- DecisionCard: decision_id: u64, decision_type: DecisionType enum, context: DecisionContext, reasoning: Vec<EvidenceItem>, outcome: Outcome, counterfactual: Option<Outcome>, timestamp: MonotonicTimestamp.
- MonotonicTimestamp: Instant::now() serialized as nanos since process start (deterministic, not wall clock).
- Query API: ledger.query(DecisionFilter { type_filter, time_range, thread_filter, symbol_filter }) -> Vec<DecisionCard>.
- Export: ledger.export_json(path) for CI analysis. Ledger survives process restart via mmap persistence.
acceptance_criteria:
1. EvidenceLedger appends 1M decision cards without crash or data loss (stress test).
2. Crash safety: kill -9 during append, restart, verify all committed cards present.
3. Query by type: returns correct subset in <1ms for 100K-card ledger.
4. Query by time range: returns correct window in <1ms.
5. JSON export produces valid, parseable output for CI consumption.
6. Monotonic timestamps increase strictly (no duplicates, no reversals).
7. Ledger overhead <100ns per append (must not become a bottleneck on allocation hot path).
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: evidence_ledger, decision_id, decision_type, outcome) per append.
- tracing::info!(target: evidence_ledger, cards_total, cards_since_last_flush, flush_size) on periodic flush.
- tracing::error!(target: evidence_ledger, append_failure, reason) on any append error.

title: Property-based testing for all ABI families (proptest + cargo-fuzz)
description:
Implement comprehensive property-based testing using proptest for structured property testing and cargo-fuzz for coverage-guided fuzzing, covering all ABI families. For each ABI family, define properties that must hold: (1) String family ŌĆö strlen(s) == manual_count(s), strcmp(a,b) == -strcmp(b,a), strcat output contains both inputs, memcpy(dst,src,n) makes dst[0..n] == src[0..n]. (2) Stdlib family ŌĆö malloc(n) returns aligned pointer or NULL, free(malloc(n)) does not crash, realloc preserves existing data. (3) Math family ŌĆö sin/cos within ULP bounds of reference, monotonicity where expected, NaN propagation. (4) Stdio family ŌĆö fprintf/fscanf round-trip preserves values, fread/fwrite round-trip preserves bytes. (5) Thread family ŌĆö mutex lock/unlock is paired, condvar signal wakes exactly one waiter. Implementation: proptest strategies for each input type (arbitrary strings, valid/invalid pointers, file descriptors). cargo-fuzz targets for each family with ASan/MSan/TSan instrumentation. CI integration: run proptest with 10K cases per property on each PR, run cargo-fuzz for 5 minutes per target nightly. Crash artifacts stored with full reproduction instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Property-based testing from Claessen & Hughes (2000) QuickCheck. Coverage-guided fuzzing from Zalewski (2014) AFL. Rust tooling: proptest crate, cargo-fuzz with libFuzzer.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Property definitions: one proptest Strategy per ABI family (e.g., arb_string(), arb_size(), arb_float()).
- proptest! macro for each property: e.g., proptest!(|(s in arb_string())| assert_eq!(strlen(s.as_ptr()), s.len())).
- cargo-fuzz targets: one per family in fuzz/fuzz_targets/. Instrumented with ASan + MSan.
- CI: proptest runs 10K cases per property on each PR. cargo-fuzz runs 5 minutes per target nightly.
- Crash artifact storage: fuzz/artifacts/ with full reproduction command in each artifact directory.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Properties defined for all 6 major ABI families (string, stdlib, math, stdio, thread, memory).
2. At least 30 properties total across all families.
3. proptest runs 10K cases per property with zero failures on current implementation.
4. cargo-fuzz targets for string, allocator, printf families with at least 1 hour total fuzzing without crashes.
5. Any crash artifact includes reproduction command that triggers the exact same failure.
6. ASan/MSan/TSan instrumentation active during fuzzing (no silent memory errors).
7. Property violation produces minimal failing input via proptest shrinking.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- proptest: CI reports property_name, cases_run, time_ms, result, shrunk_counterexample (if failed).
- cargo-fuzz: CI reports target, corpus_size, executions, coverage_pct, crashes_found.
- Crash artifacts: fuzz_crash_report.json with target, input_hex, stack_trace, sanitizer_report.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

title: CI artifact gates
description:
Implement CI artifact gates that enforce quality thresholds before any merge to main. Gates: (1) Conformance gate ŌĆö differential conformance matrix must not regress (no new failures vs previous release). (2) Performance gate ŌĆö no symbol may exceed 110% of its baseline latency at p99 (10% regression budget). (3) Safety gate ŌĆö all SOS certificates must evaluate correctly (SHA-256 hash verification + spot-check evaluation). (4) Coverage gate ŌĆö line coverage must not decrease, branch coverage must not decrease by more than 0.5%. (5) Proof gate ŌĆö all formal proof artifacts must be present and internally consistent (checksums match). (6) Evidence gate ŌĆö evidence ledger must record all safety-critical decisions made during test runs. (7) Size gate ŌĆö binary size must not exceed 110% of baseline (prevent bloat). Each gate produces a structured pass/fail report with details on what failed and why. All gates must pass for merge. Emergency override requires two approvals and creates an audit trail entry. Gate results are archived as release artifacts for traceability.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** CI quality gates from Humble & Farley (2010) Continuous Delivery. Gate design from Google SRE (2016) release engineering.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- GateRunner: CI script that evaluates all gates and produces structured pass/fail report.
- Each gate implemented as independent check with JSON output: { gate: name, result: pass|fail, details: ... }.
- Emergency override: requires --emergency-override flag + 2 approver names in commit message.
- Gate results archived as GitHub Actions artifacts with retention = 90 days.
- Binary size gate: compare stripped binary size against baseline stored in .beads/baselines/binary_size.json.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. All 7 gates implemented and running in CI pipeline.
2. Gate failure blocks merge (enforced via GitHub branch protection).
3. Emergency override creates audit trail entry in evidence ledger.
4. Gate reports parseable by CI dashboard (JSON schema validated).
5. All gates complete in <10 minutes total (parallel execution where possible).
6. False positive rate <1% (gates should not block legitimate changes).
7. Gate results accessible via gh run view for post-hoc analysis.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- Per gate: gate_name, result, duration_ms, details written to ci_gate_report.json.
- Override events: tracing::warn!(target: ci_gates, override, gate, approvers, justification).
- Historical: gate_history.json tracking pass/fail trend over last 50 runs.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.
