title: Add standalone link-run smoke harness distinct from LD_PRELOAD smoke
description: Create a standalone link/run harness that proves behavior without relying on LD_PRELOAD interposition semantics. Keep L0 interpose smoke separate from L1 standalone replacement evidence and record unsupported boundaries. Validation gate: unit tests for touched logic, fixture or e2e coverage where externally visible, structured JSONL/log evidence with bead id/mode/symbol/outcome/artifact refs, and exact closure commands. Use rch for heavy cargo gates and keep one-bead-at-a-time execution.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
1. Preserve the distinction between LD_PRELOAD interpose smoke and standalone link-run smoke; one cannot substitute for the other.
2. Define standalone smoke manifest rows with C source or fixture, link command, runtime env, replacement_level, expected loader/startup behavior, symbol/version requirements, stdout/stderr/status, and cleanup.
3. Include unit tests for manifest parsing, link command assembly, artifact isolation, expected-failure classification, stale binary rejection, and replacement-level gating.
4. Include deterministic e2e scripts that compile and run at least one minimal C program, one stdio/file program, one pthread/TLS program, one resolver/locale-sensitive program where possible, and one negative missing-obligation case.
5. Logs must include trace_id, bead_id, smoke_id, compiler, link_args, runtime_mode, replacement_level, expected_status, actual_status, loader_error, artifact_refs, source_commit, target_dir, and failure_signature.
6. Closure must list exact commands, binaries, logs, passed/blocked rows, and proof L1+ claims fail closed when standalone smoke is absent or stale.
close_reason:
Implemented standalone direct-link smoke gate distinct from LD_PRELOAD. Added tests/conformance/standalone_link_run_smoke.v1.json with 5 rows: minimal_exit, stdio_file_roundtrip, pthread_tls_errno, resolver_locale, and negative_missing_obligation. Added scripts/check_standalone_link_run_smoke.sh to validate schema, assemble direct-link commands, compile/run host baseline C fixtures, reject LD_PRELOAD substitution, reject missing/wrong/stale standalone artifacts, and emit structured JSONL with trace_id, bead_id, smoke_id, compiler, link_args, runtime_mode, replacement_level, expected_status, actual_status, loader_error, artifact_refs, source_commit, target_dir, failure_signature. Added crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/standalone_link_run_smoke_test.rs covering manifest parsing, link command assembly, artifact isolation, failure classification, stale-artifact declaration, replacement-level gating, executable script, dry-run fail-closed behavior, and run-mode baseline e2e artifacts.

Commands run: bash -n scripts/check_standalone_link_run_smoke.sh; python3 -m json.tool tests/conformance/standalone_link_run_smoke.v1.json; rustfmt --check crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/standalone_link_run_smoke_test.rs; STANDALONE_SMOKE_RUN_ID=manual-validate scripts/check_standalone_link_run_smoke.sh --validate-only; STANDALONE_SMOKE_RUN_ID=manual-dry scripts/check_standalone_link_run_smoke.sh --dry-run; STANDALONE_SMOKE_RUN_ID=manual-run scripts/check_standalone_link_run_smoke.sh --run; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/frankenlibc-bd-bp8fl-6-2-codex RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test standalone_link_run_smoke_test -- --nocapture; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/frankenlibc-bd-bp8fl-6-2-clippy RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --test standalone_link_run_smoke_test -- -D warnings; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/frankenlibc-bd-bp8fl-6-2-check RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets; git diff --check; br dep cycles --no-db --json; ubs crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/standalone_link_run_smoke_test.rs scripts/check_standalone_link_run_smoke.sh tests/conformance/standalone_link_run_smoke.v1.json.

Evidence: target/conformance/standalone_link_run_smoke.report.json and target/conformance/standalone_link_run_smoke.log.jsonl; target/standalone_link_run_smoke/manual-run/*/baseline.bin plus stdout/stderr/exit-code artifacts. manual-run passed 5/5 host-baseline C fixture binaries and blocked 10/10 strict+hardened standalone candidate rows because libfrankenlibc_replace.so is absent. claim_status remains claim_blocked, current_level remains L0, and L1+/L2/L3 standalone claims fail closed when standalone smoke is absent or stale.

Notes: full cargo fmt --check still fails on pre-existing unrelated formatting drift outside touched files; full cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings still fails on pre-existing unrelated ABI test lints such as metamorphic_mb_singlechar byte_char_slices, metamorphic_ffs unnecessary_cast, conformance_diff_priority dead_code, conformance_diff_bsd_signal function_casts_as_integer, conformance_diff_dprintf unused_unsafe, and adjacent ABI conformance-test lint debt. Thresholds: strict aggregate entrypoint target 20ns, hardened aggregate entrypoint target 200ns; direct stage targets null 1ns, TLS cache 5ns, bloom 10ns, arena 30ns, fingerprint 20ns, canary 10ns, bounds 5ns. title: EPIC: Flat Combining for Contended State [Score 6.0, section 14.2]
description:
Implement flat combining for sequentially batching operations under contention. When multiple threads contend on shared state (e.g., global allocation statistics, arena rebalancing counters, evidence ledger writes), flat combining designates one thread as the 'combiner' that executes all pending operations sequentially, eliminating lock contention. How it works: (1) Each thread publishes its pending operation to a per-thread slot in a shared publication list. (2) One thread acquires the combiner lock. (3) The combiner scans all publication slots, collects pending operations, and executes them all sequentially. (4) The combiner writes results back to each thread's slot. (5) Other threads spin-wait on their slot until the result appears. Benefits: (a) Sequential execution eliminates all synchronization within the combined batch. (b) Cache locality ŌĆö the combiner's working set stays hot. (c) Throughput scales better than lock-based approaches under high contention because the combiner amortizes lock acquisition cost across N operations. Target applications in FrankenLibC: global allocation statistics (malloc_stats/mallinfo), arena rebalancing decisions, evidence ledger batch writes, and TSM validation pipeline under burst load.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.2 of graveyard (Score 6.0). Flat combining from Hendler, Hendler & Shavit (2010 SPAA). Publication list pattern for sequential batching under contention.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FlatCombiner<T, Op, R> generic implementation in src/concurrency/flat_combining.rs.
- First application: global allocation statistics (AllocStats).
- Future applications: arena rebalancing, evidence ledger batch writes, TSM pipeline burst handling.

title: Implement flat-combining lock for global allocation stats
description:
Implement the flat-combining primitive and apply it to global allocation statistics. Core data structure: FlatCombiner<T, Op> where T is the shared state and Op is the operation enum. Components: (1) PublicationList ŌĆö array of cache-line-aligned slots, one per thread. Each slot contains: operation (Option<Op>), result (Option<Result>), active flag, age counter. (2) CombinerLock ŌĆö a simple test-and-set lock. Only the combiner holds it. (3) combine() method ŌĆö acquires lock, scans publication list, executes all pending ops on shared state T, writes results back, releases lock. (4) apply(op: Op) -> Result ŌĆö publish op to thread's slot, try to become combiner. If successful, run combine(). If not, spin-wait on slot until result appears. First application: global allocation statistics (total_allocated, total_freed, active_allocations, peak_usage, per-size-class counts). Currently protected by a Mutex<AllocStats>. Under 32-thread workload, this mutex is a top-5 contention point. Flat combining should reduce contention by 10-50x for stat-heavy workloads (e.g., mallinfo called frequently).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.2 of graveyard (Score 6.0). Flat combining from Hendler, Hendler, & Shavit (2010 SPAA). Publication list pattern from Herlihy (2008).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FlatCombiner<T, Op, R>: generic over shared state T, operation enum Op, and result type R.
- PublicationSlot: #[repr(align(128))] to span 2 cache lines (avoid false sharing on adjacent slot writes).
- Slot layout: op: AtomicPtr<Op>, result: UnsafeCell<MaybeUninit<R>>, active: AtomicBool, age: AtomicU32.
- CombinerLock: AtomicBool with try_lock() returning CombinerGuard.
- combine() scans slots, collects ops into local Vec (bounded by MAX_THREADS), executes sequentially on T, writes results back.
- apply() publishes op, tries to become combiner. If not combiner, spin_loop() on result slot with bounded spins before park().
- Thread slot assignment: thread_local! with lazy init, index from AtomicUsize::fetch_add.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. FlatCombiner<AllocStats, StatOp, StatResult> correctly computes allocation statistics under 16-thread concurrent access.
2. No lost operations: all published ops eventually executed (test: 1M ops across 16 threads, verify final stats sum).
3. Throughput >10x mutex baseline at 32 threads for write-heavy (80% write) workload.
4. Single-thread overhead <50ns per operation (combiner scan overhead amortized).
5. Fairness: per-thread throughput variance <20% (no thread starvation).
6. Publication slot memory layout verified: no false sharing (perf c2c test).
7. Works correctly when thread count exceeds slot count (graceful fallback).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: flat_combining, operation, thread_id, role=combiner|waiter, batch_size) per combine round.
- tracing::debug!(target: flat_combining, combiner_scan_time_ns, batch_size, slot_utilization) per combine.
- Periodic summary every 10K ops: total_combined, avg_batch_size, combiner_elections, wait_time_p99.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

title: Benchmark: flat combining vs lock under high contention
description:
Benchmark flat combining against mutex-based locking for global allocation statistics under various contention levels. Test matrix: (1) Thread counts: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64. (2) Operation mix: read-only (mallinfo), write-only (allocation tracking), mixed (realistic: 80% writes, 20% reads). (3) Batch sizes: 1, 10, 100, 1000 operations per thread between yield points. (4) Baselines: Mutex<AllocStats>, RwLock<AllocStats>, atomic counters (for comparison). Metrics: (a) Throughput (ops/sec aggregate). (b) Latency distribution (p50/p95/p99). (c) Combiner scan overhead (how much time spent scanning publication list). (d) Fairness (variance in per-thread throughput). (e) Cache behavior (L1/L2/L3 miss rates via perf). Expected: flat combining wins decisively at >= 8 threads for write-heavy workloads. For read-only at low thread counts, the overhead of the publication list may make simple locking faster. Document the crossover point. Generate structured JSON results + gnuplot-compatible data files for CI tracking.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.2. Benchmarking flat combining from Hendler et al. (2010). Comparison methodology from Dice, Lev, & Moir (2006).

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- BenchConfig: thread_counts, op_mixes (read-only, write-only, mixed), batch_sizes, baselines (Mutex, RwLock, atomic counters).
- Each configuration runs for 5 seconds with 2-second warmup.
- Use criterion::BenchmarkGroup with parameterized inputs.
- Atomic counter baseline: for comparison showing upper bound of lock-free performance.
- Cache analysis: perf stat -e L1-dcache-load-misses,LLC-load-misses for each configuration.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Full benchmark matrix executed: 7 thread counts x 3 op mixes x 4 batch sizes x 3 baselines = 252 data points.
2. Flat combining wins at >=8 threads for write-heavy workloads (documented crossover point).
3. Atomic counters faster for read-only at all thread counts (expected; documented).
4. Simple locking faster for 1-2 threads (expected; combiner scan overhead dominates).
5. Throughput and latency data stored as JSON + gnuplot-compatible .dat files.
6. Cache behavior data shows reduced LLC misses for flat combining vs mutex at high thread counts.
7. Gnuplot scripts committed for reproducible graph generation.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.

- Per data point: JSON with thread_count, op_mix, batch_size, baseline, ops_per_sec, p50_ns, p95_ns, p99_ns, llc_misses.
- Summary: flat_combining_benchmark.json with crossover analysis.
- Graphs: throughput_vs_threads.svg, latency_cdf.svg, cache_misses.svg generated by gnuplot scripts.

title: printf executor DSL cannot express int* output args (%n)
description:
Split off from bd-qij6l. The remaining sprintf %n case `sprintf_n_with_args` (format `%d%n`, args `[123]`) cannot be exercised by the in-process conformance executor because the fixture DSL has no way to express an `int*` output parameter. The executor therefore binds no storage for %n and the case remains tracked as a known impl gap in crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/printf_conformance_conformance_test.rs KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS.

## Fix direction
One of:
1. Extend the fixture DSL (inputs.args) to allow a typed `{"intptr_out": ...}` element that the executor materializes as a real `int` cell and whose post-call value is captured in `expected_n_value`.
2. Delete `sprintf_n_with_args` from printf_conformance.json (%n with conversions is not meaningfully testable when host glibc refuses it under FORTIFY anyway).

## Out of scope
The two no-arg %n cases (`sprintf_n_count_zero`, `sprintf_n_count_mid`) were already addressed by bd-qij6l: they now run in mode=hardened and no longer need host parity.
notes:
Closed by cc-libc. Resolved by deleting the sprintf_n_with_args fixture entirely. Rationale: host glibc refuses %n under FORTIFY, so a meaningful parity test is impossible regardless of DSL; and the no-arg %n cases (sprintf_n_count_zero/mid, covered by bd-qij6l) already exercise our impl's %n dispatch. Extending the fixture DSL with an intptr_out type would touch ~6 variadic dispatch branches in run_impl_snprintf ŌĆö not justified for one hardened-only conversion-interaction case. If we ever need pointer-out coverage, the DSL extension can be done properly as its own scope.

Validation: cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test printf_conformance_conformance_test -> 14 passed, 0 failed.

title: Printf DISC-001: Implement banker's rounding (round-half-to-even)
description:
Implement IEEE 754 round-half-to-even (banker's rounding) for %.0f and similar precision-limited float formatting. Currently uses round-half-away-from-zero which diverges from glibc.

Reference: sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) should return "2" not "3".
Spec: C11 7.21.6.1 footnote says rounding unspecified but POSIX requires IEEE 754 conformance which specifies round-half-to-even.
acceptance_criteria:
- sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 1.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 3.5) returns "4"
- All printf conformance tests pass including round_half_even
- DISC-001 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md
close_reason: FIXED: Changed format_f and format_e in printf.rs to use round_ties_even() instead of round(). Added test_format_float_bankers_rounding test. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.

title: Scanf DISC-004: Implement hex float parsing (0x1.fp+2)
description:
Implement C11 hex float format parsing for scanf %a/%A specifiers. Currently hex floats like 0x1.fp+2 (= 7.75) fail to parse.

Format: 0x[h...h][.h...h][pN] where h is hex digit, N is decimal exponent.
Spec: C11 7.21.6.2 paragraph 12 describes %a/%A hex float format.
acceptance_criteria:
- sscanf("0x1.fp+2", "%a", &f) parses correctly to 7.75
- sscanf("0x1.0p10", "%a", &f) parses to 1024.0
- sscanf("0x1p0", "%a", &f) parses to 1.0
- All scanf hex float conformance tests pass
- DISC-004 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md
close_reason: FIXED: Implemented scan_hex_float in scanf.rs to parse hex floats. Added 5 unit tests covering basic, integer, negative, negative exponent, and uppercase cases. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.

title: PORT: strpct/strspct (NetBSD libutil percentage formatters)
description: Port the NetBSD libutil percentage formatters: char *strpct(char *buf, size_t bufsize, uintmax_t num, uintmax_t denom, size_t precision) and char *strspct(char *buf, size_t bufsize, intmax_t num, intmax_t denom, size_t precision). Each renders 100 * num / denom into buf rounded to `precision` fractional digits, returning buf. denom == 0 yields "0" (or "0.0..0" for precision > 0) per NetBSD convention. strspct preserves the sign of (num * denom). Used by df/iostat/etc for "% Used" columns. Implementation: pure-safe core helper format_percent that does the integer math + rounding without floats; abi shims that snprintf the result into the bounded buffer with truncation guarded. Tests: zero/non-zero precision, denom=0, exact integers, halfway-rounding, signed strspct sign handling, undersized buffer, NULL buf returns NULL.
close_reason: shipped: strpct + strspct percentage formatters with u128 widening + integer rounding + signed/unsigned variants + 11 core tests + 12 abi tests, all green

title: fuzz_dlfcn harness: dlsym/dladdr on stale dlclose'd handle passes UB pointer to ld-linux
description: crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_dlfcn.rs Op::Sym/Op::SymFuzz/Op::Addr call dlsym on handles returned from pick_slot without checking State::Live. When a prior Close op transitioned the handle to Stale, subsequent Sym ops pass a freed handle into real libc dlsym, which dereferences it inside the dynamic loader and SEGV's. Reproducer: artifacts/fuzz_dlfcn/crash-81b48dad2c4463a9ba111dda2a7debed80d78913. Same shape as bd-2t616 (pidfd_timer), bd-reb0e (TimerDelete), earlier fuzz_socket/mmap stale fixes. Fix: skip op when h.state != State::Live. Harness bug, not a library bug.
close_reason: Fixed: Sym/SymFuzz/Addr and Close now skip stale handles. dlclose on already-closed handle corrupts glibc loader state; manifests as later-SEGV on dlsym of unrelated LIVE handle. 177K runs clean after fix.

title: REVIEW: [HIGH] pthread_cond_wait/timedwait clobbers RECURSIVE/ERRORCHECK mutex owner without ownership check
description:
In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs lines 2541-2554 (pthread_cond_wait) and 2775-2788 (pthread_cond_timedwait), for PTHREAD_MUTEX_RECURSIVE_TYPE and PTHREAD_MUTEX_ERRORCHECK_TYPE the code unconditionally clears the mutex owner (owner.store(MUTEX_NO_OWNER)) and zeros the recursive lock count (count.swap(0)), without first verifying that the calling thread is actually the owner.

If thread A holds a recursive mutex with count=N and thread B incorrectly invokes pthread_cond_wait on that same mutex (a POSIX violation, but glibc detects it for ERRORCHECK), the current code:
1. Saves count (which belongs to A) into thread B's stack-local saved_count
2. Stores NO_OWNER, destroying A's ownership record
3. Resets count to 0, destroying A's recursion depth
4. Waits on the condvar
5. On wake, claims ownership for thread B and restores A's saved count to thread B

This effectively allows thread B to silently steal the mutex. POSIX mandates EPERM for pthread_cond_wait with an unlocked or non-owned ERRORCHECK mutex.

Repro: Two threads, mutex_init with PTHREAD_MUTEX_ERRORCHECK; thread A locks; thread B calls pthread_cond_wait without locking; expect EPERM, observe owner state corruption.

Fix: Before saving/clearing owner, read current owner via owner.load(Acquire) and compare to core_self_tid(). If mismatch and type is ERRORCHECK, return EPERM. For RECURSIVE, also return EPERM (POSIX says behavior is undefined, but EPERM is safer than corruption).
close_reason: Fixed: commit 64fb99b2 added owner verification in pthread_cond_wait/timedwait for RECURSIVE/ERRORCHECK; returns EPERM for non-owner. Regression test added.

TTL via ns_format_ttl. Class: 1=IN, 2=CS, 3=CH, 4=HS, else "CLASSn". Type: A=1, NS=2, CNAME=5, PTR=12, MX=15, AAAA=28, TXT=16; else "TYPEn". Per-type rdata: A=dotted quad, AAAA=inet_ntop(AF_INET6), NS/CNAME/PTR=dn_expand from rdata, MX="pref name", TXT="\"string\" \"string\"" with backslash-escaping. Unknown types use RFC 3597 generic notation: "\\# rdlen hexbytes". Returns chars written or -1 on overflow / parse error. Both handle NULL buf via -1.

title: EPIC: Implement real DNS resolution for getaddrinfo
description:
getaddrinfo() currently only resolves:
1. Numeric IP addresses (127.0.0.1, ::1)
2. Hostnames found in /etc/hosts

It does NOT perform actual DNS queries. This means programs like wget, curl (for non-localhost URLs), dig, nslookup, and any network client that resolves hostnames will fail.

OPTIONS:
A) Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric, non-/etc/hosts hostnames
B) Implement a minimal DNS stub resolver (send UDP query to nameserver from /etc/resolv.conf)
C) Both: try our resolver first, fall back to host

Option A is fastest to implement and most reliable. Option B gives us full independence from host libc.

ALREADY DONE: /etc/hosts lookup works, numeric IP parsing works, freeaddrinfo correctly frees our Box-allocated structures.
close_reason: Already implemented. getaddrinfo has a full native DNS stub resolver: udp_dns_query() sends A/AAAA queries to /etc/resolv.conf nameservers via SYS_socket/SYS_sendto/SYS_recvfrom with configurable timeout/retry. ResolverConfig parses nameservers, search domains, ndots. Resolution chain: numeric -> /etc/hosts -> DNS -> repair fallback. 46 resolv tests pass. Previously verified when closing bd-zh1y.7 and bd-1y7.

title: Add host libc delegation for DNS-requiring getaddrinfo calls
description:
When getaddrinfo receives a hostname that:
1. Is NOT a numeric IP address
2. Is NOT found in /etc/hosts

Delegate to the host libc's getaddrinfo via our ELF resolver. The host's getaddrinfo will perform the actual DNS query.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. resolve_host_symbol_raw("getaddrinfo") to get host's function pointer
2. Call host_getaddrinfo(node, service, hints, res) 
3. Return the host's result directly (the addrinfo structs are heap-allocated by the host)
4. In freeaddrinfo, detect whether the addrinfo was allocated by us (Box) or host (malloc) and free accordingly

TRICKY PART: freeaddrinfo must distinguish our Box-allocated addrinfos from host-malloc-allocated ones. Could use a sentinel field or check if the pointer is in our Box allocation range.

title: Left-Right Sync for complex metadata reads (section 14.3)
description:
Implement Left-Right synchronization for wait-free reads of complex metadata structures in FrankenLibC. Left-Right maintains two copies of the data structure (left and right). Readers always access one copy wait-free (no locks, no atomics on the read path). Writers update the inactive copy, then toggle the active pointer, then wait for readers to drain from the old copy before updating it too. Compared to RCU (bd-3aof), Left-Right provides: (a) Wait-free reads (not just lock-free). (b) Works for non-RCU-friendly data structures (those requiring consistent snapshots of multiple fields). Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocator configuration ŌĆö the allocator's configuration (size-class table, arena count, thread-to-arena mapping) is a complex multi-field structure that must be read consistently. Left-Right provides atomic snapshot reads without locks. (2) TSM policy table ŌĆö the TSM's decision policy (state machine transitions, threshold parameters) may be updated at runtime (e.g., when switching from strict to hardened mode). Left-Right ensures readers always see a consistent policy snapshot. (3) Symbol dispatch table ŌĆö the IFUNC-like dispatch table mapping symbols to implementations. Updated when CPU features change (e.g., AVX-512 becomes available after cpuid check). Implementation: LeftRight<T> wrapper with read() -> &T (wait-free) and write(|t: &mut T| ...) (serialized, may block briefly).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
design:
**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.3 of graveyard. Left-Right from Ramalhete & Correia (2015). Wait-free reads with consistent snapshots for multi-field structures.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- LeftRight<T: Clone>: maintains two copies (left: Box<T>, right: Box<T>) with version_index: AtomicBool.
- read() -> ReadGuard<T>: returns reference to active copy. No atomic RMW on read path (single atomic load of version_index).
- ReadGuard registers in per-reader arrival/departure counters (2 counters, one per version).
- write(|t: &mut T| ...) -> (): acquires writer mutex, updates inactive copy, toggles version_index, waits for readers on old version to drain, updates other copy.
- Reader drain: spin on reader counter for old version until zero. Bounded wait if readers are well-behaved.
- T: Clone required to synchronize the two copies after toggle.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. LeftRight<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/write cycle.
2. Read path is wait-free: no CAS, no RMW (verified by cargo asm inspection).
3. Consistent snapshot: multi-field read always sees consistent state (test with 2-field struct updated atomically).
4. 16-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, TSan clean.
5. Read latency <3ns at p99 (compare with RCU at <2ns and RwLock at ~15ns).
6. Writer latency <1us including reader drain wait at p99 under 16-reader load.
7. No starvation: writer completes within bounded time even with continuous readers.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: left_right, operation=read, version, reader_count) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: left_right, operation=write, version_toggled, drain_wait_ns) per write.
- tracing::warn!(target: left_right, slow_drain, drain_wait_ns, remaining_readers) if drain exceeds 100us.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

title: PORT: inet_net_pton + inet_net_ntop (BIND/libresolv CIDR codec)
description: BIND/libresolv inet_net_pton(af, src, dst, size) parses CIDR-like network-address strings ("192.168.0/24", "10/8") into the dst byte buffer, returning the prefix length in bits, or -1 with errno on error. Companion inet_net_ntop(af, src, bits, dst, size) performs the inverse ŌĆö render network bytes + prefix into "192.168.0/24" form. AF_INET only (libresolv ships AF_INET6 for inet_net_ntop but pton is AF_INET-only). Used by ifconfig, route, DNSSEC tools, and anything dealing with IPv4 prefixes. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::inet::net_pton with parse_cidr returning Result<(prefix_bits, [u8; 4]), NetPtonError> + format_cidr returning Vec<u8>. Thin abi shims in glibc_internal_abi.rs since libresolv exports them.

title: Kernel: Blackwell approachability controller (design safe set + payoff vector)
description:
Design a Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective routing.

Goal:
- Guarantee that the cumulative payoff vector (latency, risk, coverage, etc.) approaches a safe set under the chosen policy.

Design tasks:
- Define payoff vector components using existing cached signals.
- Define target safe set (convex polytope) per mode.
- Define arms/actions (Fast vs Full; Repair vs Deny thresholds).
- Choose update rule that is O(1) and integer-friendly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Written derivation of approachability update + how it improves over ad-hoc thresholds.

title: REVIEW: [HIGH] futex_wait_timed panics (debug) / wraps to effectively-infinite timeout (release) on i64 subtraction overflow when deadline.tv_sec < 0
description:
Location: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1101-1104 (futex_wait_timed).

Current code:
    let deadline = unsafe { &*abstime };
    let mut rel = libc::timespec {
        tv_sec: deadline.tv_sec - now.tv_sec,
        tv_nsec: deadline.tv_nsec - now.tv_nsec,
    };

Defect:
- `absolute_timespec_valid` at line 1075 only checks `tv_nsec` in [0, 1e9); it does NOT check tv_sec >= 0 nor that deadline is after now.
- `deadline.tv_sec - now.tv_sec` is an unchecked i64 subtraction.
- In debug builds: `i64::MIN - 1_700_000_000` PANICS with 'attempt to subtract with overflow', aborting the process.
- In release builds: the subtraction silently wraps to a huge positive value (~9.2e18), defeating the subsequent `rel.tv_sec < 0` check and handing the kernel a timespec that corresponds to ~3e11 years ŌĆö the call then blocks effectively forever instead of returning ETIMEDOUT.

Reachability: Every pthread_cond_timedwait / pthread_cond_clockwait / sem_timedwait / pthread_mutex_timedlock that eventually routes through futex_wait_timed. An adversarial or buggy caller passing tv_sec=i64::MIN (or any extreme negative) aborts the process in debug mode or loses the timeout contract in release.

Severity: HIGH. Exported libc surface, crashes from user input on common code paths.

Fix plan:
- Use i64::checked_sub for the tv_sec subtraction. Treat overflow or a deadline in the past (rel.tv_sec < 0 after carry) uniformly as ETIMEDOUT.
- Also tighten absolute_timespec_valid to reject tv_sec < 0 so the invariant is visible at the API boundary.
- Add a regression test: pthread_cond_timedwait with tv_sec=-1 must return ETIMEDOUT (not hang, not crash).
close_reason: Fixed: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs absolute_timespec_valid now rejects tv_sec < 0 in addition to out-of-range tv_nsec, and futex_wait_timed uses i64::checked_sub on the deadline-minus-now arithmetic so an extreme tv_sec (including i64::MIN) produces ETIMEDOUT instead of panicking (debug) or wrapping to a ~9.2e18-second effectively-infinite timeout (release). Added two regression tests in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_abi_test.rs: cond_timedwait_rejects_negative_tv_sec (tv_sec = -1 and i64::MIN) and cond_timedwait_rejects_out_of_range_tv_nsec (tv_nsec in {-1, 1_000_000_000, i64::MAX}). Run: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_review cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test -> 183/0 passed in 0.27s.

title: PORT: fchmodat2 + eventfd2 + 4 rt_sig* (rt_sigprocmask/queueinfo/suspend/tgsigqueueinfo)
description: Add 6 more Linux libc syscall wrappers. (1) fchmodat2(dirfd, pathname, mode, flags) -> int (Linux 6.6+, SYS_fchmodat2=452) ŌĆö like fchmodat but accepts AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW + AT_EMPTY_PATH flags. (2) eventfd2(initval, flags) -> int (SYS_eventfd2=290) ŌĆö kernel-name variant of eventfd that accepts flags directly; some sandboxes/seccomp policies trace this name explicitly. (3) rt_sigprocmask(how, *set, *oldset, sigsetsize) -> int (SYS_rt_sigprocmask=14). (4) rt_sigqueueinfo(tgid, sig, *uinfo) -> int (SYS_rt_sigqueueinfo=129) ŌĆö send a siginfo_t to a thread group. (5) rt_sigsuspend(*mask, sigsetsize) -> int (SYS_rt_sigsuspend=130). (6) rt_tgsigqueueinfo(tgid, tid, sig, *uinfo) -> int (SYS_rt_tgsigqueueinfo=297) ŌĆö send a siginfo_t to a specific thread. Skip rt_sigaction (kernel struct layout differs from libc) and rt_sigreturn (kernel signal-trampoline; never callable from user code). Forward via libc::syscall + raw_syscall_with_errno bridge; NULL-pointer guards return EFAULT where applicable.

Goal: prevent architectural drift.

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text:
## Epic Notes: Inventory + Drift Control

### Why We Need This
This repo is intentionally ambitious: lots of runtime_math modules and many moving parts. Drift is the default failure mode.

We must continuously ensure:
- AGENTS.md module inventory matches code reality
- decide() implementation matches the documented decision law
- snapshot schemas are stable and versioned
- fused signals are consistent across modules

### What This Epic Produces
- automated checks (compile-time + tests) that fail loudly on drift
- a wiring checklist so adding kernels doesnŌĆÖt silently omit snapshots/tests
- conservative merge rules for decision-law changes (behavior proofs required)

### Definition Of Done
- We can answer: ŌĆ£why did runtime choose FullValidate here?ŌĆØ with a stable policy_id + evidence.
- A new runtime_math module canŌĆÖt land without:
  - being registered
  - being snapshotted
  - being represented in fusion signals (if relevant)
  - being covered by at least one test

Schema versioning, cadence classification, fusion consistency, decision-law validation all delivered.

title: syscall: io_uring sqpoll veneer parity
Track A: Implement _IO_iter_* native stream iteration over NativeStreamRegistry
description:
## Goal
Replace 4 stub _IO_iter_* functions in io_internal_abi.rs (lines 1430-1457) with real implementations that iterate over the NativeStreamRegistry.

## Current State
All 4 functions return null, making the stream list appear empty:
- _IO_iter_begin() -> null (should return first occupied slot index)
- _IO_iter_end() -> null (should return sentinel past last slot)
- _IO_iter_file(iter) -> null (should return FILE* for the slot)
- _IO_iter_next(iter) -> null (should advance to next occupied slot)

## Why This Matters
glibc atexit/fflush-all path may walk _IO_list via these iterators. If the list is empty, exit-time flushing of native streams is silently skipped by any code using glibc's _IO_iter API. Our native fflush(NULL) works via NativeStreamRegistry.flush_all(), but external code (atexit handlers in host glibc) that walks _IO_list via iterators would miss our streams.

## Design
Use slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding:
- _IO_iter_begin: Lock registry, find first OCCUPIED slot (index 0..255), return (index+1) cast to *mut c_void. Return null if no occupied slots. We use index+1 so that 0 (stdin at slot 0) is distinguishable from null.
- _IO_iter_end: Return (STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY+1) cast to *mut c_void as sentinel. This is 257, always > any valid encoded slot.
- _IO_iter_file: Decode (iter as usize - 1) to get slot index, call registry.get(index), return &NativeFile as *mut c_void. Return null if index is invalid or slot is empty.
- _IO_iter_next: Decode current slot index from iter, scan forward for next OCCUPIED slot, return encoded index or end sentinel.

### Thread Safety
The registry is behind a Mutex. Each iterator call acquires and releases the lock independently. This means the iteration is NOT atomic ŌĆö streams could be added/removed between calls. This matches glibc behavior (glibc's _IO_iter functions are also not atomic without _IO_list_all_lock held externally).

### Implementation Helpers
Add to NativeStreamRegistry:
- first_occupied() -> Option<usize>: scan slots[0..] for first SLOT_OCCUPIED
- next_occupied(after: usize) -> Option<usize>: scan slots[after+1..] for next SLOT_OCCUPIED

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs: Replace 4 function bodies, add 2 helpers to NativeStreamRegistry

## Unit Tests (in tests/io_internal_native_file_test.rs)
- test iter_begin_returns_first_stream: Register 0 extra streams -> begin iterates over stdin/stdout/stderr (3 entries)
- test iter_begin_end_empty_after_unregister: Unregister all stdio, begin == end (no streams)
- test iter_file_returns_valid_native_file: iter_file on valid iterator returns non-null with correct fd
- test iter_file_on_end_returns_null: iter_file on end sentinel returns null
- test iter_next_advances_to_next_occupied: skip empty slots correctly
- test iter_next_at_end_returns_end: advancing past last stream returns end sentinel
- test iter_full_traversal_count: register 5 streams, iterate begin->next->next...->end, count matches open_count()
- test iter_survives_concurrent_unregister: unregister a stream during iteration, no crash

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_io_iter_native.sh
Build and run a focused Rust test binary that:
1. Opens 10 files via our native fopen (if available) or memfd_create
2. Walks _IO_iter_begin -> _IO_iter_next until _IO_iter_end
3. Counts entries and verifies >= 10
4. Emits structured JSONL trace per step

## Structured Logging
Each function emits: trace_id, api_family=io_internal, symbol=_IO_iter_begin|end|file|next, slot_index, result_ptr, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 _IO_iter_* functions return real iterators over NativeStreamRegistry
- 8+ unit tests pass covering positive, negative, edge, and concurrency cases
- e2e script passes
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (58 passes) not regressed
close_reason: Implemented _IO_iter_begin/end/file/next over NativeStreamRegistry using slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding. Added first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers. 8 new integration tests: begin returns first stream, end is constant sentinel, file returns valid NativeFile, file on end/null returns null, next past end stays at end, full traversal visits all streams, traversal after unregister skips freed slots. All 40 io_internal tests pass. Clippy clean.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Implementation note: NativeStreamRegistry already has get() and open_count(). Use slot index as opaque pointer (usize cast to *mut c_void). Sentinel for _IO_iter_end = STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY (256). Lock registry mutex during scan. Consider exposing first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers on NativeStreamRegistry.

title: [HIGH] rpc_abi_test parallel race: free(): invalid size SIGABRT at --test-threads>=4 (locally reproducible bd-ye0f4 sibling)
description:
## Symptom

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test under default cargo
parallelism aborts with free(): invalid size:

    running 35 tests
    RPC: Success
    RPC: Timed out
    RPC: Unknown error
    free(): invalid size
    error: test failed, to rerun pass -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test
    process didn't exit successfully: .../rpc_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)

The 3 RPC lines come from clnt_perrno_does_not_crash, which runs
early. Abort fires AFTER those lines, meaning another parallel-
running test is the culprit, not clnt_perrno itself.

## Bisection (LOCAL ŌĆö unlike bd-ye0f4 which only reproduced on rch)

    --test-threads=1: 35 passed, 0 failed
    --test-threads=2: 35 passed, 0 failed
    --test-threads=4: SIGABRT with free(): invalid size
    --test-threads=8: SIGABRT with free(): invalid size (default)

Locally reproducible on a 64-core x86_64 host at HEAD 17431c7a ŌĆö
threshold is exactly at --test-threads=4. This is the first
locally-reproducible sample of the bd-ye0f4 cluster's
'free(): invalid size' signature. Previous cluster beads (bd-g2bd5,
bd-ye0f4) only reproduced under rch remote execution with the
identical glibc error.

## Why this matters

The bd-ye0f4 cluster has been cycling fixŌåÆregress since
2026-04-22 across a581f0ac, e289f838, b04d7238 (all partial TLS
dtor scoping fixes). Every round left the race live. Root cause
has been elusive because nobody could reproduce it locally ŌĆö only
on rch workers with specific worker scheduling. This rpc_abi_test
sample is a deterministic local reproducer: same
'free(): invalid size' glibc detection, pinpoints the race at the
4+ thread boundary, and runs every time.

## Diagnosis candidates

clnt_perrno_does_not_crash routes through crate::unistd_abi::write.
write() fans into runtime_policy::decide (complex state machine
with potentially-racy internal globals) and runtime_policy::observe
(recording validations). Other tests in the same binary exercise
rpc_thread_createerr, rpc_thread_svc_*, rpc_thread_tls_* ŌĆö thread-
local getters that allocate buffers.

The most likely explanation: one test's thread-local RPC state is
freed by another thread's teardown (thread-local cross-allocator
free), or runtime_policy has a per-thread AtomicI32 / RefCell that
gets torn-down by one test while another test is mid-decide.

## Fix direction

1. Re-run with RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=3 (already tried, no
   extra info ŌĆö glibc's native free() abort is silent). Next level:
   compile with RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address' (nightly) and rerun
   the 4-thread test to get an ASan stack trace pointing at the
   freeing site and the allocating site.
2. Audit all thread-locals in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
   and crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs for allocation
   Drop impls that could free pointers allocated by a different
   thread's allocator. rpc_abi.rs has SPERRNO_BUF and
   SPCREATEERR_BUF (Cell<[u8; N]>) which are inline arrays ŌĆö safe.
   But __rpc_thread_createerr, __rpc_thread_svc_fdset,
   __rpc_thread_svc_max_pollfd, __rpc_thread_svc_pollfd return
   pointers into allocations ŌĆö those are the likely culprits.
3. Check the 3+ rpc_thread_* tests: if they allocate in one test
   thread and the Drop fires on the next test thread (pthread-local
   rebinding), we get cross-allocator free.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test -- --test-threads=2
  -> 35 passed, 0 failed.
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test -- --test-threads=4
  -> SIGABRT, free(): invalid size.

No commit changes needed to repro. Provides the long-awaited local
sample for the bd-ye0f4 cluster.
notes:
## ROOT-CAUSE FIX by cc-libc (2026-04-22)

Bisected by running rpc_abi_test with progressively narrower test-name filters at --test-threads=4. Convergence:

    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test 'host2netname_' -- --test-threads=2  -> SIGABRT free(): invalid size
    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test host2netname_creates_valid_name -- --test-threads=4  -> 1/1 pass
    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test host2netname_null_defaults     -- --test-threads=4  -> 1/1 pass

Either test alone passes; the two together at N>=2 abort. They share no test-side state ŌĆö but they BOTH allocate via host2netname() and BOTH free via libc::free().

The actual bug at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs:2874:

    let buf = unsafe { crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc(name.len()) } as *mut c_char;

host2netname allocates through OUR internal arena (raw_alloc), but the test releases the buffer via libc::free (host glibc's free). In debug builds (test context, no LD_PRELOAD), libc::free binds at link time to glibc's free ŌĆö which sees a chunk header from our arena, interprets it as a glibc chunk-size field, gets nonsense, and aborts with 'free(): invalid size'.

The race is just chunk-layout entropy: with 1 thread the same allocator context is reused so chunks happen to look fine; with 2+ threads the arena's per-thread chunk metadata diverges from glibc's expectations and the free hits a bad size word deterministically.

## Fix

Change host2netname to allocate via libc::malloc instead of our internal raw_alloc. POSIX defines host2netname as 'returns a buffer the caller must free()' ŌĆö that means the caller's free() (glibc's in non-preload context, our interposed one in preload context) must match the alloc. libc::malloc satisfies both: in preload context it IS our interposed malloc, in non-preload context it's glibc's, and either way it pairs correctly with libc::free.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test (default parallelism) -> 35 passed, 0 failed (was SIGABRT). All thread counts 1..8 now pass.

## Wider implication for the bd-ye0f4 cluster

30+ other call sites use the same `crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc` pattern and return the buffer to the caller (inet_abi.rs, dirent_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, more in rpc_abi.rs, wchar_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, string_abi.rs). Each is a candidate sibling failure. Every test that exercises one of those entrypoints AND frees with libc::free is a potential next bd-dqqh1. Filed as an umbrella bead for systematic audit.
close_reason: Fixed: host2netname now uses libc::malloc instead of internal raw_alloc; alloc/free pair consistent with caller libc::free in both LD_PRELOAD and non-preload contexts.

title: fuzz: printf/snprintf format-string adversarial invariants (width/precision/specifier chaos)
description:
## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_printf_adversarial.rs covering format-string **security** properties beyond what fuzz_printf already does. fuzz_printf focuses on correctness of well-formed specifiers with structured args; this target covers **attacker-controlled format strings**: huge widths/precisions, positional-arg reordering, %n refusal, bracket-bomb '%*.*' chains, and buffer-exhaustion safety.

## Archetype
1 crash-detector + invariant (security contract):
- snprintf with huge width (1M) must not OOM or overrun dst buffer
- %n must either be honored with a valid ptr OR return -1 / refuse ŌĆö never crash or write to garbage
- positional '%99$d' with missing args must return an error rather than reading OOB stack

## Inputs
struct PrintfAdversarialFuzzInput {
  format: Vec<u8>,           // arbitrary bytes ŌĆö fuzzer can generate '%' + junk
  int_args: [i32; 8],        // padded arg vector
  str_args: [Option<Vec<u8>>; 4],
  dst_size: u16,
}

## Invariants
- Return value <= dst_size - 1 (or -1)
- Written bytes fit in dst_size (guard-byte sentinel check)
- Determinism on repeated calls with identical args
- No panic on ANY format byte sequence (including partial '%' at NUL, unknown specifier, repeated length modifiers)

## Expected bug classes (per CVE history)
- glibc CVE-2023-25139: %n within width-modifier chains
- glibc CVE-2010-0296: formatted-printing stack exhaustion for deeply nested positional refs
- Integer overflow in computed width (we widened i32 width to c_int during bd-0u5fm but the WIDTH field inside pattern '%2147483648d' is still parsed ŌĆö test with decimal-string widths that overflow usize)
- Off-by-one in our own buffer-truncation path

## Relation to existing work
bd-m40be (fnmatch), bd-7rxtm (strftime), bd-luc3d (length-modifier ABI) already found format-parser bugs via fuzzing. This target fills the final gap: the printf pipeline itself, beyond the structured-fixture-case harness.

## Score
5 ├Ś 4 ├Ś /2 Ōēł 10 ŌĆö CVE-class severity (format-string attacks remain a top-10 web-server vector).
notes:
Closed by cc-libc.

Delivered crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_printf_adversarial.rs with 8 archetypes covering the CVE classes listed in the bead:

- adv_parser_panic: arbitrary-byte inputs to parse_format_string / parse_format_spec (termination + bounded-segment invariant).
- adv_huge_dynamic_width: %*d with attacker-chosen width int.
- adv_bracket_bomb: %*.*d chaining width and precision magnitudes.
- adv_bracket_bomb_string: %*.*s variant with attacker-supplied string.
- adv_percent_n_refusal: %n under hardened mode ŌĆö must be honored (count==2) or refused (rc<0 or count==-1), never write to garbage.
- adv_overflow_width_literal: integer-overflow widths encoded as compile-time literals (%2147483648d, %99999999999d, %.99999999999d, %9999999999.9999999999d) so we never pass attacker-controlled formats to a C variadic call.
- adv_positional_out_of_range: %99$d with only one arg ŌĆö must not read past supplied varargs.
- adv_percent_storm: pure-% format string ŌĆö parser stress.

All archetypes use 64-byte GUARD_BYTE sentinels on both sides of the destination buffer; each call asserts the guards are untouched after return. Hardened mode is set once globally via sync::Once.

Safety: the target never passes an attacker-controlled format to a variadic C call (that would be UB at the language level). Attacker chaos lands in the numeric args, in the Rust parser entrypoints, or in a fixed set of compile-time format literals.

Wired into crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml as `fuzz_printf_adversarial` bin.

Validation: cargo check --bin fuzz_printf_adversarial clean (13s on vmi1149989); cargo clippy --bin fuzz_printf_adversarial --no-deps -- -D warnings clean (local fallback because remote worker lacks nightly-2026-04-13).

title: PORT: mergesort + heapsort (BSD libc sort variants)
description: BSD libc mergesort(base, nmemb, size, compar) and heapsort(base, nmemb, size, compar) ŌĆö sort variants of qsort with the same signature except they return int (0 on success, -1 with errno on failure). mergesort is STABLE (preserves input order for equal elements); heapsort is NOT stable but uses no auxiliary storage. Both are ~50 years old, present in libbsd 0.0/0.3, used by mtree, ls -t, sort -s, and any tool that needs stability without writing its own merge sort. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::sort::{mergesort, heapsort} that operate on byte slices via index permutation (so the existing qsort comparator-callback infrastructure works unchanged) + abi shims that return 0/-1 with ENOMEM on alloc failure (mergesort) or EINVAL on bad inputs.
close_reason: shipped: core::stdlib::sort {mergesort (stable, copy-out + timsort), heapsort (in-place max-heap)} + stdlib_abi shims with EINVAL/ENOMEM mapping; 13+9 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built

title: [HIGH] fuzz gap: signal/socket/fortify/setjmp/mmap/dlfcn/pthread/c11threads ABI modules are unfuzzed (mmap now partially covered by fuzz_mmap)
description:
## Motivation

/testing-fuzzing skill discovery pass on crates/frankenlibc-abi/src enumerated 3646 `pub unsafe extern "C" fn` symbols across 38 modules and cross-referenced against the 30 fuzz bins registered in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml. The CVE-class gap is concentrated in 8 modules that currently have **no** fuzz target:

| Priority | Module | Symbols | CVE class |
|---------|--------|---------|----------|
| 1 | mmap | 11 | memory-safety (UAF / OOB / W^X bypass) ŌĆö **covered by fuzz_mmap in same PR** |
| 2 | signal | 29 | handler setup races, async-signal-safety, privilege escalation |
| 3 | socket | 18 | network attack surface, kernel ABI complexity |
| 4 | fortify | 79 | `__*_chk` overflow-detection wrappers ŌĆö if broken, they silently fail to protect |
| 5 | setjmp | 12 | control-flow integrity, sigsetjmp edge cases |
| 6 | dlfcn | 7 | dlopen/dlsym attack surface |
| 7 | pthread | 141 | concurrency (mutex/cond/rwlock/barrier) ŌĆö best done as TSan campaign |
| 8 | c11threads | 21 | C11 threading (thrd_/mtx_/cnd_) ŌĆö same pattern as pthread |

## Status of priority-1 (mmap)

Delivered in the same push: `crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_mmap.rs` ŌĆö stateful harness with a live-mapping arena, 7 ops (Map/Unmap/Protect/Sync/Advise/Remap/MarkStaleInPlace), guard-byte sentinels on writable mappings, alignment invariant on mmap/mremap return addresses, and the use-after-unmap contract (every op on a Stale slot must return -1 / MAP_FAILED). The harness intentionally exercises PROT_EXEC / RWX requests against the hardened-mode membrane to cover the expected refuse-or-clamp path. Registered as `fuzz_mmap` bin. Validation: cargo build + cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings both clean.

## Fix direction for the remaining 7

Each should follow the pattern already established by the 5 fuzz targets cc-libc shipped earlier this session (fuzz_printf_adversarial, fuzz_pathname, fuzz_env, fuzz_sched, fuzz_pidfd_timer):

- Stateful archetype with a handle table where applicable (signal ŌåÆ signal handler slots; socket ŌåÆ sockfds; pthread ŌåÆ thread / mutex / cond handles; setjmp ŌåÆ jmp_buf storage slots).
- Return-code contract + lifecycle invariant (stale handle ŌåÆ must fail).
- Direct syscall differential where possible (signal, socket via libc::syscall). For pthread/c11threads, use a BTreeMap / counter shadow model because the ABI deviates from raw syscall shape.
- Safety bounds: signal restricted to {0, SIGWINCH, SIGURG} the way fuzz_pidfd_timer does; socket in PF_LOCAL with SOCK_DGRAM + AT_EMPTY_PATH style so no real network traffic.
- TSan-specific campaign for pthread/c11threads; run with `RUSTFLAGS=-Zsanitizer=thread`.

## Score priority

Per /testing-fuzzing skill, score = (untrusted-input) * (complexity) * (unsafe/native) * (prior CVEs). The mmap surface scores highest because of 40+ years of mmap/munmap/mprotect CVEs (CVE-2017-1000405 ptrace race, CVE-2016-5195 'Dirty COW' mprotect interaction, CVE-2023-40283 vma-use-after-free). Signal is second (CVE-2020-14386 af_packet, CVE-2021-44733 tee), fortify third (CVE-2023-4911 'Looney Tunables' via __stack_chk_* interaction).

## Exit criteria

One child bead per remaining module, each:
- Delivers a fuzz target in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/
- Registers it in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml
- Achieves Ōēź1000 exec/s (skill Hard Rule 1) or, for stateful harnesses, Ōēź100 exec/s
- Cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings clean
- Seed corpus committed (exclude artifacts per existing .gitignore)
notes: Fuzz coverage delivered for all 8 priority modules + 4 pthread sub-targets: fuzz_mmap, fuzz_signal, fuzz_socket, fuzz_fortify, fuzz_setjmp, fuzz_dlfcn, fuzz_pthread_mutex, fuzz_pthread_cond, fuzz_pthread_keys, fuzz_pthread_rwlock, fuzz_pthread_sync_misc, fuzz_c11threads. All 12 sources present + registered in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml. Verified via rch full build (exit 0, 62s remote) + fuzz_signal 30s smoke (2.4M runs, ~77k exec/s, 0 crashes). fuzz_xdr added later in this session covers the 9th high-CVE surface (SunRPC XDR) ŌĆö found and fixed bd-bhqcn, bd-lc5ss, bd-xpgak in that target.

title: PORT: statmount/listmount + finit_module + quotactl_fd + map_shadow_stack + bpf + kexec_load + kexec_file_load (8 modern Linux syscalls)
description: Add 8 modern Linux libc syscall wrappers, all forwarding via libc::syscall + raw_syscall_with_errno{,_long} bridges. (1) statmount(*req, *out, bufsize, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 457) ŌĆö query a single mount by ID into struct statmount. (2) listmount(*req, *out, count, flags) -> int (Linux 6.8+, syscall 458) ŌĆö enumerate child mount IDs into a buffer. (3) finit_module(fd, *args, flags) -> int (SYS_finit_module=313) ŌĆö load a kernel module from an open file descriptor. (4) quotactl_fd(fd, cmd, id, *addr) -> int (Linux 5.14+, SYS_quotactl_fd=443) ŌĆö like quotactl but takes an open fd instead of a block-device path. (5) map_shadow_stack(addr, size, flags) -> long (Linux 6.6+, syscall 453) ŌĆö allocate a CET shadow stack on x86_64. (6) bpf(cmd, *attr, size) -> int (SYS_bpf=321) ŌĆö universal BPF interface. (7) kexec_load(entry, nr_segments, *segments, flags) -> long (SYS_kexec_load=246) ŌĆö load a new kernel image for kexec reboot. (8) kexec_file_load(kernel_fd, initrd_fd, cmdline_len, *cmdline, flags) -> long (SYS_kexec_file_load=320) ŌĆö same but from open fds. NULL-pointer guards where applicable; opaque pointers for kernel structs whose layouts are version-specific.
close_reason: shipped: 8 modern Linux syscall wrappers (statmount, listmount, finit_module, quotactl_fd, map_shadow_stack, bpf, kexec_load, kexec_file_load) + 10 abi tests covering NULL guards + privilege/kernel fallbacks

title: PORT: dehumanize_number (NetBSD human-readable size parser)
description: NetBSD dehumanize_number(str, *size_out) parses a C string of optional whitespace + optional sign + digits + optional one-char suffix (bBkKmMgGtTpPeE for 1024^k multipliers, where b/B = 1) into an int64_t. Returns 0 on success (writes through *size), -1 with errno = EINVAL for malformed input or ERANGE for arithmetic overflow. Used by NetBSD ftpd/mtree/dd-conv and other tools for human-readable size limits in config files. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::dehumanize_number::parse returning Result<i64, DehumanizeError> with Invalid/Overflow variants + thin abi shim that maps the error categories to errno + writes the value through the caller pointer.
close_reason: shipped: core::stdlib::dehumanize_number + stdlib_abi shim; 28+10 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built

title: syscall: close_range invalid flags on reversed bounds should still return EINVAL
description: Host glibc returns EINVAL for close_range(5, 4, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags plus reversed bounds stay in the exact EINVAL bucket.

title: wordexp parity: quote-aware badchar and command-substitution semantics
description:
Background:
- `wordexp` is marked Implemented, but current parsing is too naive about quoting/escaping context.
- Host glibc accepts quoted and escaped metacharacters literally and only rejects true unquoted shell separators.

Goal:
- Bring the native `wordexp` implementation into parity with host glibc for a bounded shell-lexing slice.

Deliverables:
1) Quote-aware/escape-aware bad-character validation for `wordexp`.
2) Quote-aware command-substitution detection so `WRDE_NOCMD` only rejects real command substitution.
3) Host-parity ABI tests for quoted semicolons, escaped separators, quoted command substitution literals, and bare bad-char cases.

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test wordexp_ -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings
close_reason: Match glibc wordexp quote/escape badchar and WRDE_NOCMD semantics with host-parity tests

title: resolv: extend async-DNS ABI tests with explicit GNU mode constants and badflags expectations
description: The current unistd_abi async-DNS tests exercise degenerate success cases but not GNU mode semantics. Add explicit GAI_WAIT/GAI_NOWAIT/badflags constants in the ABI test layer and verify errno/output contracts for both valid and invalid mode combinations.
close_reason: Extended async-DNS ABI tests with explicit GNU mode and badflags constants and verified the invalid-mode errno contract against host libc.

title: PORT: bsearch_r (NetBSD reentrant bsearch with thunk)
description: Port the NetBSD `void *bsearch_r(const void *key, const void *base, size_t nmemb, size_t size, int (*compar)(const void *, const void *, void *), void *thunk)` reentrant binary search. Same as POSIX bsearch but the comparator receives an extra `void *thunk` parameter so callers can carry context (e.g. case-insensitive flag, sort key index) without a global. Implementation: thin abi shim implementing standard binary search via the existing memory layout helpers; compar invoked with (key, mid_elem, thunk). NULL key/base/compar returns NULL; size==0 or nmemb==0 returns NULL. Tests cover found / not-found, leftmost/rightmost positions, NULL guards, thunk passes through unchanged.
close_reason: shipped: bsearch_r reentrant binary search with thunk + 6 abi tests, all green

title: syscall: renameat2 invalid flags should override null oldpath EFAULT with EINVAL
description: Host glibc returns EINVAL for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, ".", 0x80000000), so invalid flags dominate the null-source-path fault. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.

title: Conformance: add execute_sprintf_case for printf_conformance
Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build
description:
`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. However, `scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh` only scans `*_abi.rs` files, missing `host_resolve.rs` entirely. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.

To fix:
1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).
2. Update `build.rs` to respect `source_scan_exceptions` so `host_resolve::dlvsym` (and any future exceptions) can be whitelisted.
close_reason: Current tree already aligned: build.rs, replacement guard, and standalone cargo check all agree

title: abi tests: synchronous_gai_wrappers flakes under --test-threads=16 (fmemopen/tmpfile fixed)
description:
## Symptom

Three additional unit tests flake intermittently under high
parallelism (--test-threads=16, ~25% combined hit rate across 12
sweep iterations), distinct from bd-7v4oe (closed):

| Test | Hit rate (12 sweeps) | File |
|------|---------------------|------|
| `synchronous_gai_wrappers_match_host_degenerate_contracts` | 3/12 (25%) | DNS/network |
| `tmpfile_closes_without_leaking_named_tmp_entries` | 1/12 (~8%) | filesystem |
| `fmemopen_write_creates_stream` | 1/12 (~8%) | stdio |

The bd-7v4oe fix (registry RAII guard) verified its own target
flake is gone in this same sweep ŌĆö these are separate races.

## Likely categories

1. **synchronous_gai_wrappers** ŌĆö touches `getaddrinfo` and friends
   which read /etc/resolv.conf, /etc/hosts, etc. Under parallelism
   the host glibc resolver may serialize on shared file descriptors
   or DNS cache state, producing different EAI_* codes than
   "degenerate" baseline. Fix candidate: serialize the test (or
   subset) with a shared lock.
2. **tmpfile_closes_without_leaking_named_tmp_entries** ŌĆö POSIX
   `tmpfile()` creates an unlinked file in /tmp. Under parallel
   pressure the directory enumeration check may see entries from
   other in-flight `tmpfile()` calls. Fix candidate: use a unique
   subdirectory or accept transient races in the leak count check.
3. **fmemopen_write_creates_stream** ŌĆö fmemopen builds a memory-
   backed FILE*. Could share registry state with other stream
   tests. Fix candidate: same RAII registry-cleanup pattern as
   bd-7v4oe.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3) ŌĆö test-only flakes that don't affect production
behavior. Worth fixing because they pollute CI and mask real
regressions in stress sweeps. The bd-7v4oe pattern (RAII cleanup
guards) likely applies to (3); (1) and (2) need targeted analysis
of the host syscall surface they exercise.
notes:
Partial fix: fmemopen flake closed in dd82f863 (STREAM_REGISTRY_PROBE_LOCK), tmpfile flake closed in 18baf908 (drop racy redundant /tmp enumeration). synchronous_gai_wrappers flake remains open ŌĆö needs cross-test DNS state serialization across multiple test binaries (host glibc gai async queue shares state across all in-process callers). Reproduces only when running --tests (multiple binaries), not within unistd_abi_test alone. 10/10 isolated runs pass.

Fully fixed: three-part fix in this cycle.
1. GAI_TEST_LOCK (31f5b9f2): intra-binary serialization across 16 gai_*/getaddrinfo_a tests. Single-pass parity now stable.
2. GaicbShape struct size (9d4bd2f5): test's GaicbShape was 32 bytes but glibc's struct gaicb is 56 (adds __return + __unused[5]). host_gai_error reads __return ŌĆö with the short struct it read uninitialized stack past the boundary, producing divergent host-vs-abi results under parallel pressure. Real ABI-correctness bug surfaced by the flake.
3. xdr_reference calloc (fd7c7cba): bonus fix. Stress uncovered a SIGABRT / misaligned-pointer deref in xdr_reference_depth_limit regression test; root cause was libc::malloc leaving inner pointer fields uninitialized for recursive procs. Switched to libc::calloc (mirrors glibc xdr_reference's malloc+memset idiom).
Verified: 5/5 xdr_abi_test --test-threads=16 iters pass; earlier gai 7/8 iters had zero gai failures (xdr SIGSEGV was the #3 bug, now also fixed). Full abi 8x stress in flight.

title: Cross-cutting: franken_kernel crate integration (Cx, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId)
description:
Integrate FrankenLibC with franken_kernel suite substrate: adopt canonical Cx capability context, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types. All TSM decisions must flow through Cx. All evidence records must use canonical IDs. Must not fork franken_kernel schema. ADOPTION WEDGE: FrankenLibC must function standalone without franken_kernel (fallback to local types).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.
notes:
### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.
close_reason: Delivered: code + artifacts present.

close_reason: Harness shipped; DISC-ERR-001 documented.

title: FIX: err.h progname source ŌĆö use argv[0] basename, not /proc/self/comm (DISC-ERR-001)
description: Discovered by err.h diff harness. fl::err_abi::get_progname() reads /proc/self/comm which the kernel truncates to TASK_COMM_LEN=15 chars and which is per-task (forked children inherit, then any prctl(PR_SET_NAME) shifts it). glibc reads __progname which is the basename of argv[0] ŌĆö full executable name. Result: fl warns/errs print 'conformance_dif: ...' instead of 'conformance_diff_err_h-30ba6209...: ...'. Fix: capture argv[0] in __libc_start_main (already done elsewhere?), expose as a global, basename-strip in get_progname.
close_reason: Use argv0-derived program name for err.h prefixes

title: Exit-time segfault in LD_PRELOAD mode
notes: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: glibc's internal _IO_list_all not patched during LD_PRELOAD. Binaries with COPY relocations work; others crash. glibc's exit path (_IO_flush_all_lockp) iterates its own _IO_list_all with corrupted vtables. FIX: dlvsym glibc's _IO_list_all at startup and set to NULL or our valid list. Confirmed _exit bypasses crash, main-return crashes.

title: PORT: unvis (NetBSD vis(3) streaming byte decoder)
description: NetBSD vis(3) streaming decoder unvis(*cp, c, *astate, flag) ŌĆö single-byte state-machine inverse of vis(3). Caller feeds bytes one at a time; the function returns UNVIS_VALID (1) when *cp holds a fully decoded byte, UNVIS_VALIDPUSH (2) when *cp is decoded AND the current input byte should be re-fed, UNVIS_NOCHAR (3) for partial sequences, UNVIS_SYNBAD (-1) for malformed input. The opaque *astate carries decoder state across calls. Used by mtree, BSD pkg tools, and anything needing a streaming decoder rather than the all-at-once strunvis. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdio::vis::UnvisDecoder state machine + thin abi shim that maps the i32 state cell to/from the typed enum.

title: PORT: strenvisx/strsenvisx (NetBSD env-aware vis(3) variants)
description: Port the NetBSD vis(3) env-aware variants: strenvisx(dst, src, srclen, flags, cerr_ptr) and strsenvisx(dst, dlen, src, srclen, flags, extra, cerr_ptr). These are extended forms of strvisx/strsvisx that additionally consult the VIS_OPTIONS environment variable for comma-separated flag names (e.g. "VIS_OCTAL,VIS_NL,VIS_TAB,VIS_CSTYLE,VIS_SP,VIS_NOSLASH,VIS_HTTPSTYLE,VIS_GLOB,VIS_HTTP1808,VIS_MIMESTYLE,VIS_NOLOCALE,VIS_SAFE,VIS_DQ,VIS_SHELL,VIS_META") and OR the parsed bits into the caller-supplied flags. They also write a per-call character-set error indicator into *cerr_ptr (always 0 for our byte-stream-safe port). Implementation: a small parser maps known token names to existing VIS_* constants, ignores unrecognized tokens; abi shims read VIS_OPTIONS via libc::getenv and apply the OR-merge before delegating to the existing encoder. Tests: env-var bits are honored, NULL/empty env-var is a no-op, unrecognized tokens are ignored, cerr_ptr writes 0, NULL cerr_ptr tolerated.

title: PORT: __libc_csu_init / __libc_csu_fini (glibc startup stub no-ops)
description: Port the glibc startup-stub symbols __libc_csu_init(int argc, char **argv, char **envp) and __libc_csu_fini(void). These were emitted by older glibc versions (<2.34) as part of the program-startup code generated by gcc/ld; the entry point calls __libc_csu_init before main and __libc_csu_fini after exit to drive .init_array / .fini_array constructors. Modern glibc inlines these into _start, but statically-linked binaries built with older toolchains still reference the symbols and fail at link time without them. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust no-op stubs ŌĆö our runtime drives constructors / __cxa_atexit handlers via separate machinery, so the csu shells have nothing to do. Tests verify the symbols resolve and the calls return without panic.
close_reason: shipped: __libc_csu_init / __libc_csu_fini glibc startup-stub no-ops + 3 abi tests, all green

title: PORT: __cxa_pure_virtual (Itanium C++ ABI pure-virtual stub)
description: Port the Itanium C++ ABI `__cxa_pure_virtual()` entry point. C++ compilers populate vtable slots for pure virtual functions with a pointer to this symbol; it gets invoked at runtime if a caller dispatches through an under-construction or partially-destructed object whose vtable still points to a pure-virtual stub. Per Itanium ABI / glibc convention, the implementation writes "pure virtual method called" + "terminate called without an active exception" diagnostics to stderr and calls abort(). Without this symbol, every C++ binary linked against our libc fails at link time with an undefined reference. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" fn that writes the diagnostic via libc::write to fd 2 and invokes libc::abort(). Tests cover the symbol resolves (via fork+execute test that calls __cxa_pure_virtual in the child and verifies the child terminates via SIGABRT with the expected stderr message).
close_reason: shipped: __cxa_pure_virtual Itanium C++ ABI stub writing diagnostic + abort + 1 fork-based abi test verifying SIGABRT termination, all green

title: [CRITICAL] glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption under default parallelism (free(): invalid size)
description:
## Symptom

`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` aborts with:

```
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
...
process didn't exit successfully: ...glibc_internal_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)
```

Reproduced on two independent workers (vmi1149989, vmi1156319) with current main tip 7c9b3127 on 2026-04-22.

## Key diagnostic

The same binary with `--test-threads=1` **passes all 172 tests cleanly** (finishes in 0.04s). Corruption only manifests when the default rayon/cargo test parallelism is enabled. That narrows the bug to:

- a data race on a heap-allocated global / thread-local
- two tests freeing the same allocation through different paths
- or a reallocation that trusts a size field modified concurrently

## Location hint

Failure in the two runs landed near these test names (the test that actually freed the corrupt pointer is not necessarily the one that emitted the abort ŌĆö whichever thread allocated last is what glibc trips on):

run 1 (vmi1149989): last 'ok' line was `resolv_context_native_shim_reuses_tls_context_and_preserves_errno`, then `free(): invalid size`.

run 2 (vmi1156319): last 'ok' line was `test_idna_from_dns_null_input`, then `free(): invalid size`.

Cluster of tests running around the failure: TLS dtors (`test_call_tls_dtors_*`), IDNA (`test_idna_*`), resolv context shims (`resolv_context_native_shim_*`, `resolv_context_override_*`). These all touch thread-local state / TLS dtor lists and share a registry.

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=2` to get a glibc backtrace pointing at the freeing site.
2. Audit the TLS-dtor list, resolv-context override pool, and IDNA allocation paths for any `Box::from_raw` / `free` on a pointer whose size metadata is modified elsewhere without a lock.
3. Add `#[ignore]` or `serial_test::serial` gate on the implicated tests only after the root cause is understood (applying `serial` prematurely masks the bug instead of fixing it).

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace` from completing cleanly ŌĆö the very first workspace-wide test abort halts CI before later crates (frankenlibc-core, frankenlibc-membrane, frankenlibc-harness, frankenlibc-bench) ever run. Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE pass.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` reproduces reliably on two distinct workers.
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test -- --test-threads=1` passes (172/172, 0.04s).
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
close_reason: Scoped __cxa_thread_atexit_impl capture to local TLS dtor tests and revalidated glibc/startup ABI suites

title: inet_addr returns host-order u32 instead of network-order

comments.author: Dicklesworthstone
comments.text: frankenlibc_core::inet::inet_addr uses u32::from_be_bytes(octets) which returns the host-endian integer value (0x7F000001 = 2130706433 for 127.0.0.1). glibc inet_addr returns the same 4 bytes stored as a native u32 in network byte order (0x0100007F = 16777343 on LE). Fix: change from_be_bytes to from_ne_bytes in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Detected by conformance differential execution.

comments.author: PurpleHawk
comments.text: Fixed inet_addr to return network byte order. Changed u32::from_be_bytes to u32::from_ne_bytes in frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Updated 5 tests that incorrectly used to_be_bytes() to use to_ne_bytes(). Also fixed pre-existing pthread compilation error: added tls_values field to ThreadHandle and wired up register_thread_tls call with the correct 2-arg signature. All 61 inet tests pass.
